<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/tools/copilot-claude-proxy.md`

```text
1	# Copilot → Claude Code proxy
2	
3	Back [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) with the
4	**Claude models from a GitHub Copilot subscription**, via a local
5	reverse-engineered proxy — the maintained fork
6	[`caozhiyuan/copilot-api`](https://github.com/caozhiyuan/copilot-api)
7	(npm `@jeffreycao/copilot-api`). The original
8	[`ericc-ch/copilot-api`](https://github.com/ericc-ch/copilot-api) is
9	officially unmaintained ([issue #233](https://github.com/ericc-ch/copilot-api/issues/233)
10	points at the fork) but still works via `COPILOT_API_PKG=copilot-api@0.7.0`.
11	Both packages share the same token file
12	(`~/.local/share/copilot-api/github_token`), so switching needs **no re-auth**.
13	
14	- **Shell helpers**: `~/.config/shell/43_copilot_proxy.sh` (`copilot-proxy`, `claude-copilot`, `claude-copilot-once`, `copilot-run`, `copilot-here`, `copilot-model`)
15	- **Runner**: `bunx @jeffreycao/copilot-api` (pinned; matches the `bunx` convention in `07_bunx_cli.sh`)
16	- **Not installed by ansible** — pulled on demand via `bunx`, so it stays off the
17	  provisioning path.
18	
19	!!! warning "This violates GitHub Copilot's Terms of Service"
20	    Using a Copilot subscription to power a non-GitHub agent is not permitted, and
21	    copilot-api is reverse-engineered/unofficial. It can trigger GitHub's
22	    **abuse detection** and lead to **temporary suspension of Copilot access**.
23	    Claude Code is token-hungry (frequent background calls, large context) —
24	    note the fork has **no rate limiter** (see Gotchas); `COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET=1`
25	    reduces background chatter. Use at your own risk; prefer a personal account
26	    over a corporate seat.
27	
28	## Quick start
29	
30	```sh
31	copilot-proxy auth      # one-time: GitHub device login (stores a ghu_ token)
32	
33	claude-copilot          # one-off session on the proxy (auto-starts it; no file writes)
34	claude-copilot-once     # pin THIS project, run one session, then auto-unpin (proxy must be up)
35	
36	copilot-here on         # OR: pin THIS project — plain `claude` uses the proxy
37	copilot-here off        # unpin — back to the real Anthropic backend
38	```
39	
40	## How it works
41	
42	```
43	Claude Code ──Anthropic /v1/messages──▶ copilot-api (localhost:4141)
44	                                          │ native /v1/messages passthrough
45	                                          │ Authorization: Bearer <copilot token>
46	                                          ▼
47	                                   api.githubcopilot.com  (your Copilot sub)
48	```
49	
50	- Claude Code speaks only the **Anthropic Messages API** (`/v1/messages`).
51	- The fork prefers Copilot's **native Anthropic-style `/v1/messages`
52	  endpoint** when available (Enterprise plans:
53	  `api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com/v1/messages`) and only falls back to
54	  translating through the OpenAI-compatible `/chat/completions`. Native
55	  passthrough is what fixes **extended-thinking blocks**, **WebSearch**
56	  (routed through a Responses-capable GPT model, default `gpt-5-mini`), and
57	  the **infinite tool-retry loops** the original's Anthropic→OpenAI→Anthropic
58	  translation caused.
59	- The original (`copilot-api@0.7.0`) always translates through
60	  `/chat/completions` — thinking blocks are dropped and WebSearch doesn't work.
61	- Claude Code is pointed at the proxy via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` — injected either
62	  as per-process env (`claude-copilot`) or via the gitignored
63	  `./.claude/settings.local.json` (`copilot-here on`). See "Settings-layer design".
64	
65	## Settings-layer design (which file gets the proxy config, and why)
66	
67	Claude Code merges settings low → high: `~/.claude/settings.json` (user) →
68	`./.claude/settings.json` (project, committed) → `./.claude/settings.local.json`
69	(local, gitignored) → CLI flags. The
70	[docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings) (and third-party writeups)
71	claim shell env vars beat every settings-file `env` block, but **empirically
72	(verified 2026-07) current Claude Code lets the `settings.local.json` `env`
73	block beat inherited shell env** — see Gotchas.
74	
75	Two of those layers are already owned by other tooling and must stay clean:
76	
77	| Layer | Owner | Why proxy config must NOT go here |
78	|---|---|---|
79	| `~/.claude/settings.json` | chezmoi (`dot_claude/modify_settings.json`) | always-on for *every* project; fights the chezmoi merge |
80	| `./.claude/settings.json` | `claude-plans-here` (`plansDirectory`) | committed to git — proxy config would leak to the team |
81	
82	So the proxy uses the two layers nobody else owns:
83	
84	| Enable | Mechanism | Scope | Disable |
85	|---|---|---|---|
86	| `claude-copilot` / `copilot-run` | per-process env vars | one session | just run plain `claude` next time |
87	| `claude-copilot-once` | `./.claude/settings.local.json` pin, auto-reverted | one session | automatic — unpins on exit |
88	| `copilot-here on` | `./.claude/settings.local.json` (gitignored) | this project, sticky | `copilot-here off` |
89	
90	```
91	~/.claude/settings.json          .claude/settings.json         shell env                    .claude/settings.local.json
92	(chezmoi: hooks/plugins)    <    (git: plansDirectory)     <   (claude-copilot)         <   (copilot-here on/off)
93	```
94	
95	(Shell env still beats the user- and project-level settings files, so
96	`claude-copilot` works everywhere `copilot-here` is off — it just cannot
97	*override* an active `copilot-here` pin.)
98	
99	## Shell helpers
100	
101	### `copilot-proxy [start|stop|restart|status|logs [N]|whoami|auth]`
102	
103	Manages the background proxy on `$COPILOT_PROXY_PORT` (default `4141`).
104	
105	| Env var | Default | Meaning |
106	|---|---|---|
107	| `COPILOT_PROXY_PORT` | `4141` | port the proxy listens on |
108	| `COPILOT_API_PKG` | `@jeffreycao/copilot-api@1.13.14` | `bunx` package spec (pin / upgrade; `copilot-api@0.7.0` = old original) |
109	| `COPILOT_PROXY_RATE` | `15` | `--rate-limit` seconds — **original package only** (the fork has no rate limiter) |
110	| `COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET` | `0` | `1` = inject extra quota-saving Claude Code env (see below); off by default because it slightly degrades the UX |
111	
112	Set these in `~/.shellrc.adhoc` (or the per-shell secrets files). `start` refuses
113	to run until `copilot-proxy auth` has stored a token, and waits up to ~20s for the
114	proxy to answer before returning. `start` detects the package flavor from
115	`COPILOT_API_PKG`: only the exact original `copilot-api` gets
116	`--rate-limit`/`--wait` (the fork's `start` doesn't have those flags).
117	
118	`copilot-proxy whoami` is the real login check: it prints your plan / quota
119	(fails loudly if the token is missing or expired). On the fork it queries the
120	running proxy's `/usage` endpoint (jq-summarized) and falls back to
121	`bunx <pkg> debug` when the proxy is down; on the original it runs
122	`check-usage`. Use it instead of eyeballing the token file — the token is a
123	plaintext credential and should not be opened in an editor.
124	
125	### `claude-copilot [--no-specstory] [claude args...]` — one-off session
126	
127	Layer 1: run a single Claude Code session on the proxy with **zero file
128	writes**. Auto-starts the proxy if it isn't answering, then launches `claude`
129	with the `ANTHROPIC_*` env injected per-process (shell env beats the user- and
130	project-level settings files — but **not** an active `copilot-here` pin in
131	`settings.local.json`; see Gotchas).
132	
133	- Wraps in `specstory run claude` when specstory is installed (markdown
134	  auto-save — same convention as `scode`/`svibe`); opt out with
135	  `--no-specstory`. Extra args reach the claude CLI via specstory's
136	  `-c "custom command"` passthrough: `claude-copilot -c` → continue session.
137	- Revert = nothing to revert; plain `claude` next time is untouched.
138	
139	### `claude-copilot-once [--no-specstory] [claude args...]` — one-shot pinned session
140	
141	Layer 1's ephemerality with Layer 2's reliability: pin **this project** via
142	`copilot-here on`, run one `claude-copilot` session, then `copilot-here off` on
143	exit — even on Ctrl-C. Use it when pure env injection isn't enough because
144	`settings.local.json` outranks shell env (see Gotchas), but you don't want to
145	leave a sticky pin behind.
146	
147	- **Precondition:** the proxy must already be running — unlike `claude-copilot`
148	  this does **not** auto-start it; it prints a `copilot-proxy start` hint and
149	  returns non-zero if the proxy isn't answering.
150	- **Prior-pin safe:** if `copilot-here` is already `on` here, the existing pin is
151	  left in place on exit (nothing is unpinned that you didn't ask for).
152	- The session itself is just `claude-copilot "$@"`, so specstory auto-save,
153	  `--no-specstory`, and `-c` (continue) all work the same way.
154	- On exit it reminds you the proxy is still up and how to `copilot-proxy stop`.
155	
156	### `copilot-run <cmd...>` — generic env injector
157	
158	The building block under `claude-copilot`: auto-starts the proxy and runs *any*
159	command with the proxy env. Useful for other Anthropic-compatible tools or a
160	custom specstory invocation:
161	
162	```sh
163	copilot-run specstory run claude    # exactly what claude-copilot does
164	copilot-run claude --resume         # raw claude, no specstory
165	```
166	
167	### `copilot-here [on|off|status]` — sticky per-project toggle
168	
169	Layer 2: pin **this project** to the proxy via `./.claude/settings.local.json`
170	so plain `claude` (and `scode`/`svibe` panes, which just run
171	`specstory run claude`) uses the proxy until you turn it off. Requires `jq`.
172	
173	- `on` — jq-merges the proxy `env` block into `settings.local.json` (creates it
174	  if missing) and makes sure git ignores the file (via `.git/info/exclude`;
175	  Claude Code only auto-gitignores files *it* creates). The committed
176	  `.claude/settings.json` (`plansDirectory` etc.) is never touched.
177	- `off` — removes exactly the env keys `on` added; other content you put in
178	  `settings.local.json` survives, and the file is deleted if it becomes empty.
179	- `status` — pinned? which base URL / model? warns when the proxy isn't running.
180	
181	### `copilot-model [<id>|-l|-c]`
182	
183	Switches the pinned Copilot model. Requires `jq`. Write target — never the
184	committed `.claude/settings.json`:
185	
186	- `copilot-here` is ON in the current project → edits
187	  `./.claude/settings.local.json`.
188	- otherwise → writes the global state file
189	  `~/.local/state/copilot-proxy/model`, which `claude-copilot`, `copilot-run`
190	  and the next `copilot-here on` pick up. (`$COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL` overrides
191	  the state file; final fallback is `claude-opus-4-8[1m]`.)
192	
193	Behavior:
194	
195	- Fuzzy id: `copilot-model opus-4-8` resolves to `claude-opus-4-8`; dotted
196	  input is normalized (`opus-4.8` works too).
197	- A `[1m]` suffix (`copilot-model 'opus-4-8[1m]'`) is stripped for validation
198	  and re-appended — it's a Claude Code-only 1M-context hint, see the model-id
199	  section below.
200	- Validated against the live proxy `/v1/models` (falls back to a static Claude
201	  list if the proxy is down); typos and ambiguous prefixes are rejected.
202	- No argument → `fzf` picker. `-c` prints the current model and which layer it
203	  came from.
204	- Writes both `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` and `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL` —
205	  **the change only takes effect on the next `claude` launch** (env is read at
206	  startup). Switching model does **not** require restarting the proxy.
207	
208	## Injected env (what both layers set)
209	
210	```json
211	{
212	  "env": {
213	    "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL": "http://localhost:4141",
214	    "ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN": "dummy",
215	    "ANTHROPIC_MODEL": "claude-opus-4-8[1m]",
216	    "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL": "claude-opus-4-8[1m]",
217	    "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL": "claude-sonnet-5[1m]",
218	    "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL": "claude-haiku-4-5",
219	    "ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL": "claude-haiku-4-5",
220	    "CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC": "1"
221	  }
222	}
223	```
224	
225	`ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` is ignored by the proxy but must be set.
226	`CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC=1` cuts background chatter (helps with
227	rate limits). Do **not** paste this into the committed `.claude/settings.json` —
228	use `copilot-here on` instead.
229	
230	With `COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET=1` (opt-in, default off), both layers additionally
231	inject the fork-README quota savers:
232	
233	| Extra env | Effect | UX cost |
234	|---|---|---|
235	| `CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER=0` | no billing/version info in system prompts → avoids prompt-cache invalidation | none noticeable |
236	| `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_PROMPT_SUGGESTION=false` | no prompt-suggestion calls | no suggestions |
237	| `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY=0` | no away-summary calls | no away summaries |
238	| `DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS=1` | fewer background model calls | fewer niceties (haiku flavor text etc.) |
239	
240	`copilot-here off` removes these keys regardless of the current
241	`COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET` value.
242	
243	## Gotchas (these cost real debugging time)
244	
245	### `settings.local.json` env beats shell env (docs say otherwise)
246	
247	Verified empirically (2026-07): current Claude Code lets the `env` block of
248	`./.claude/settings.local.json` **override inherited shell env vars** — the
249	opposite of what the official settings docs imply. Consequences:
250	
251	- `claude-copilot` / `copilot-run` **cannot** redirect a project where
252	  `copilot-here on` is active (harmless when both point at the same proxy,
253	  silently wrong when they don't).
254	- Trialing another proxy/port via the wrapper requires `copilot-here off`
255	  first.
256	
257	### The fork has no rate limiter
258	
259	The fork's `start` dropped `--rate-limit`/`--wait`. Its README's mitigation is
260	reducing Claude Code's chatter instead — that's exactly what
261	`COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET=1` injects (off by default here; we prioritize UX over
262	Copilot quota). If you really want request throttling, fall back to the
263	original: `COPILOT_API_PKG=copilot-api@0.7.0`.
264	
265	### Fork wart: `context_management` can 400
266	
267	Newer Claude Code context-editing may inject `context_management`, which
268	Copilot's native `/v1/messages` endpoint rejects with a 400
269	([caozhiyuan#305](https://github.com/caozhiyuan/copilot-api/issues/305),
270	version-dependent). Real Claude Code runs didn't trigger it in testing, but if
271	you see unexplained 400s on long sessions, check that issue first.
272	
273	### Do not use Claude Code's `/model` picker
274	
275	It sends Anthropic's *official* dated ids (e.g. `claude-opus-4-8-YYYYMMDD`),
276	which the Copilot backend rejects:
277	
278	```
279	API Error: 400 {"error":{"message":"The requested model is not supported.",
280	"code":"model_not_supported", ...}}
281	```
282	
283	Pin the model with `copilot-model` instead — undated hyphenated ids
284	(`claude-opus-4-8`) work; only the picker's dated ids fail.
285	
286	### Dotted ids cause the "[Opus 4] retired" warning and a >100% context HUD
287	
288	Historic gotcha, fixed by the hyphenated defaults. With a **dotted** id
289	(`claude-opus-4.8`, the only shape the original proxy accepted), Claude Code
290	fails to match its built-in model table, so it:
291	
292	- displays `[Opus 4]` and warns *"Claude Opus 4 was retired"* (falls back to
293	  the nearest known, retired name), and
294	- assumes a **200k** context window, while Copilot actually serves opus-4-8 /
295	  sonnet-5 with **1M** (`max_context_window_tokens: 1000000` in `/v1/models`)
296	  — so HUD/statusline context can read >100% and compaction triggers on the
297	  wrong budget.
298	
299	The fix is the id shape the helpers now inject by default:
300	**`claude-opus-4-8[1m]`** — hyphenated so Claude Code recognizes the family
301	(correct display name, no retirement warning), plus the `[1m]` suffix so it
302	sizes context to 1M. Claude Code strips `[1m]` before sending, so the proxy
303	sees a valid id (a *literal* `...[1m]` in a raw API call is rejected —
304	`copilot-model` handles the stripping when validating).
305	
306	### The token gotcha: `gho_` vs `ghu_`
307	
308	There are two different GitHub tokens, and they are **not** interchangeable:
309	
310	| Source | Prefix | `copilot_internal/v2/token` exchange |
311	|---|---|---|
312	| OpenCode's stored auth | `gho_` | **fails (404)** |
313	| `copilot-proxy auth` (device login) | `ghu_` | **works** |
314	
315	OpenCode's `gho_` token (OAuth App) works only when used *directly* as a Bearer
316	against `api.githubcopilot.com`; it cannot complete copilot-api's classic
317	token-exchange step. **Let `copilot-proxy auth` mint its own `ghu_` token — do not
318	reuse OpenCode's.** Token is stored at `~/.local/share/copilot-api/github_token`.
319	
320	## Available Claude model ids
321	
322	Verified via `/v1/models` + `/v1/messages` (2026-07): `claude-opus-4-5`,
323	`claude-opus-4-6`, `claude-opus-4-7`, `claude-opus-4-8`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`,
324	`claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-sonnet-5`, `claude-haiku-4-5`. The fork accepts
325	both hyphenated and legacy dotted (`claude-opus-4.8`) forms at request time,
326	but **use hyphenated ids in Claude Code** — dotted ids break its model
327	detection (see Gotchas). Context windows from `/v1/models` capabilities:
328	opus-4-8 and sonnet-5 are **1M** (`max_prompt_tokens: 936000`), haiku-4-5 is
329	200k — append `[1m]` to the 1M models so Claude Code knows. Non-Claude models
330	(gpt-5.5, gemini-3.1-pro-preview, …) are also served — see `copilot-model -l`
331	or `GET /v1/models`.
332	
333	## Useful commands
334	
335	```sh
336	claude-copilot                       # one-off proxy session (specstory-wrapped)
337	claude-copilot-once                  # one-shot session via the settings.local.json pin (auto-reverted)
338	copilot-here status                  # is this project pinned to the proxy?
339	copilot-model -c                     # current model + which layer it came from
340	copilot-proxy status                 # up? which Claude models?
341	copilot-proxy whoami                 # validate token → account / plan / quota
342	copilot-proxy logs 60                # tail the proxy log
343	# usage dashboard (bundled locally by the fork):
344	#   http://localhost:4141/usage-viewer?endpoint=http://localhost:4141/usage
345	```
346	
347	## See also
348	
349	- [Copilot embeddings → semantic search](copilot-embeddings.md) — the same proxy's
350	  `/v1/embeddings` endpoint wired into `copilot-embed` + `semsearch` (local semantic search)
351	- [caozhiyuan/copilot-api](https://github.com/caozhiyuan/copilot-api) — the
352	  maintained fork this setup runs (npm `@jeffreycao/copilot-api`)
353	- [ericc-ch/copilot-api](https://github.com/ericc-ch/copilot-api) — the
354	  unmaintained original ([#233](https://github.com/ericc-ch/copilot-api/issues/233));
355	  still usable via `COPILOT_API_PKG=copilot-api@0.7.0`
356	- [`bunx` CLI aliases](../shells/aliases.md#copilot--claude-code-proxy)
357	- [Claude Code settings precedence](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings) — why
358	  `settings.local.json` / env vars are the right injection layers
359	- [Agent overlays](agent-overlays.md) — the chezmoi-managed `~/.claude/settings.json`
360	  this design deliberately stays out of
361	- OpenCode's native GitHub Copilot provider (no proxy needed for OpenCode itself)
362
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 11:06:06Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/shell/43_copilot_proxy.sh`

```text
1	# 43_copilot_proxy.sh - GitHub Copilot -> Anthropic/OpenAI proxy for Claude Code
2	#   (shared bash + zsh).
3	#
4	# Runs the maintained copilot-api fork (caozhiyuan/copilot-api, npm
5	# @jeffreycao/copilot-api) so a GitHub Copilot subscription can back Claude Code
6	# (and any OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible client). The original ericc-ch/copilot-api
7	# is unmaintained (its issue #233 points at the fork) but still works via
8	# COPILOT_API_PKG=copilot-api@0.7.0 — flags differ per package, see
9	# _copilot_pkg_flavor. Both packages share the same token file, so switching
10	# needs no re-auth. Full guide + risks: docs/tools/copilot-claude-proxy.md.
11	#
12	# Public surface:
13	#   copilot-proxy [start|stop|status|restart|logs|whoami|auth]   - manage the proxy
14	#   copilot-run <cmd...>       - run any command with the proxy env injected
15	#                                (auto-starts the proxy first)
16	#   claude-copilot [args...]   - one-off Claude Code session on the proxy
17	#                                (specstory-wrapped when available; zero file writes)
18	#   claude-copilot-once [args...] - one-shot session via the copilot-here pin
19	#                                (settings.local.json; auto-reverted, even on Ctrl-C)
20	#   copilot-here [on|off|status] - sticky per-project toggle via the gitignored
21	#                                ./.claude/settings.local.json (never touches the
22	#                                committed .claude/settings.json)
23	#   copilot-model [<id>|-l|-c] - switch the pinned model (edits settings.local.json
24	#                                when copilot-here is on, else the global state file)
25	#
26	# Settings-layer design (why settings.local.json / env vars, and NOT the
27	# committed .claude/settings.json nor ~/.claude/settings.json):
28	#   - ~/.claude/settings.json is chezmoi-managed (dot_claude/modify_settings.json)
29	#     and would make the proxy always-on for every project.
30	#   - ./.claude/settings.json is committed (claude-plans-here puts plansDirectory
31	#     there) — proxy config must not leak into git.
32	#   - Claude Code precedence (low→high): user < project < settings.local.json
33	#     (gitignored) < CLI flags. GOTCHA (verified 2026-07): current Claude Code
34	#     lets the settings.local.json env block BEAT inherited shell env vars —
35	#     so copilot-run/claude-copilot cannot override a project where
36	#     `copilot-here on` points elsewhere (run `copilot-here off` first).
37	#
38	# Portability notes (POSIX subset, both shells source this file):
39	#   - runs the proxy via `bunx` (matches 07_bunx_cli.sh); pinned version avoids a
40	#     per-launch @latest registry round-trip. Override with COPILOT_API_PKG.
41	#   - no ZLE/compdef/setopt/glob-qualifiers here (bash would error on source).
42	#
43	# Env (set in ~/.shellrc.adhoc or ~/.config/{zsh/secrets.zsh,bash/secrets.sh}):
44	#   COPILOT_PROXY_PORT   default: 4141        - port the proxy listens on
45	#   COPILOT_API_PKG      default: @jeffreycao/copilot-api@1.13.14
46	#                                             - bunx package spec (pin/upgrade;
47	#                                               copilot-api@0.7.0 = old original)
48	#   COPILOT_PROXY_RATE   default: 15          - --rate-limit seconds; ONLY used
49	#                                               by the original package (the fork
50	#                                               has no rate limiter)
51	#   COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET  default: 0           - 1 = inject extra quota-saving env
52	#                                               (fewer background calls, but a
53	#                                               slightly degraded Claude Code UX)
54	
55	# --- shared constants / helpers -------------------------------------------------
56	
57	_copilot_port() { printf '%s' "${COPILOT_PROXY_PORT:-4141}"; }
58	_copilot_pkg()  { printf '%s' "${COPILOT_API_PKG:-@jeffreycao/copilot-api@1.13.14}"; }
59	
60	# CLI flavor from the package spec: the ORIGINAL bare `copilot-api` takes
61	# --rate-limit/--wait and has `check-usage`; the fork (and anything else)
62	# doesn't. Strips a trailing @version, but not the @scope/ prefix.
63	_copilot_pkg_flavor() {
64	  local name; name="$(_copilot_pkg)"
65	  case "$name" in
66	    copilot-api|copilot-api@*) printf 'original' ;;
67	    *) printf 'fork' ;;
68	  esac
69	}
70	_copilot_base() { printf 'http://localhost:%s' "$(_copilot_port)"; }
71	_copilot_logfile() { printf '%s' "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/copilot-api-$(_copilot_port).log"; }
72	_copilot_pidfile() { printf '%s' "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/copilot-api-$(_copilot_port).pid"; }
73	
74	# --- throttle shim (optional, in front of the fork) -----------------------------
75	# A tiny Bun reverse proxy that caps concurrent in-flight upstream requests and
76	# transparently retries 403/429 (GitHub enterprise abuse throttling) BEFORE any
77	# body streams — so downstream agents never see "Please run /login". Toggle with
78	# `copilot-proxy shim on|off`; tune via COPILOT_SHIM_{PORT,MAX,RETRIES,BACKOFF_MS}.
79	_copilot_shim_port()    { printf '%s' "${COPILOT_SHIM_PORT:-4142}"; }
80	_copilot_shim_base()    { printf 'http://localhost:%s' "$(_copilot_shim_port)"; }
81	_copilot_shim_script()  { printf '%s' "${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/shell/copilot-throttle-shim.js"; }
82	_copilot_shim_logfile() { printf '%s' "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/copilot-shim-$(_copilot_shim_port).log"; }
83	_copilot_shim_pidfile() { printf '%s' "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/copilot-shim-$(_copilot_shim_port).pid"; }
84	_copilot_shim_state()   { printf '%s' "${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/copilot-proxy/shim"; }
85	
86	# Enabled? env COPILOT_PROXY_SHIM overrides a persisted on/off state file.
87	_copilot_shim_enabled() {
88	  case "${COPILOT_PROXY_SHIM:-}" in
89	    1|on|true|yes)  return 0 ;;
90	    0|off|false|no) return 1 ;;
91	  esac
92	  local sf; sf="$(_copilot_shim_state)"
93	  [ -f "$sf" ] && [ "$(command head -n1 "$sf" 2>/dev/null)" = "on" ]
94	}
95	
96	# Is the shim port answering at all? (curl w/o -f: any HTTP reply = exit 0, so a
97	# 502 from a fork-down passthrough still counts as "shim process is up".)
98	_copilot_shim_alive() {
99	  command curl -s -o /dev/null --max-time 2 "$(_copilot_shim_base)/v1/models" >/dev/null 2>&1
100	}
101	
102	# Base URL Claude Code should talk to: the shim when it's enabled AND actually
103	# up, otherwise the fork directly (never break just because the shim is down).
104	_copilot_client_base() {
105	  if _copilot_shim_enabled && _copilot_shim_alive; then _copilot_shim_base; else _copilot_base; fi
106	}
107	
108	# Base URL for PERSISTENT pins (copilot-here settings.local.json): the shim when
109	# it's enabled (it's auto-started with the proxy, so it'll be up when in use),
110	# else the fork. Not gated on currently-alive since the file outlives this shell.
111	_copilot_pinned_base() {
112	  if _copilot_shim_enabled; then _copilot_shim_base; else _copilot_base; fi
113	}
114	
115	# Start the shim (idempotent). Points it at the fork; inherits COPILOT_SHIM_*.
116	_copilot_shim_start() {
117	  if _copilot_shim_alive; then return 0; fi
118	  if ! command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
119	    printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: shim needs 'bun' (via mise) — skipping." >&2
120	    return 1
121	  fi
122	  local script; script="$(_copilot_shim_script)"
123	  if [ ! -f "$script" ]; then
124	    printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: shim script not found at $script" >&2
125	    return 1
126	  fi
127	  COPILOT_SHIM_PORT="$(_copilot_shim_port)" COPILOT_SHIM_UPSTREAM="$(_copilot_base)" \
128	    nohup bun "$script" >"$(_copilot_shim_logfile)" 2>&1 &
129	  printf '%s\n' "$!" >"$(_copilot_shim_pidfile)"
130	  local i=0
131	  while [ "$i" -lt 10 ]; do
132	    _copilot_shim_alive && return 0
133	    sleep 1; i=$((i + 1))
134	  done
135	  printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: shim did not come up — check $(_copilot_shim_logfile)" >&2
136	  return 1
137	}
138	
139	# Stop the shim.
140	_copilot_shim_stop() {
141	  local pidf; pidf="$(_copilot_shim_pidfile)"
142	  if [ -f "$pidf" ]; then
143	    local pid; pid="$(command cat "$pidf" 2>/dev/null)"
144	    [ -n "$pid" ] && kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null
145	    command rm -f -- "$pidf"
146	  fi
147	  command pkill -f "copilot-throttle-shim.js" 2>/dev/null
148	  return 0
149	}
150	
151	# Global default-model state file (used by copilot-run/claude-copilot when the
152	# project has no copilot-here pin). Written by `copilot-model`.
153	_copilot_model_state() {
154	  printf '%s' "${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/copilot-proxy/model"
155	}
156	
157	# Resolve the model for env injection: $COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL > state file > default.
158	#
159	# Model-id shape matters (verified 2026-07):
160	#   - HYPHENATED ids (claude-opus-4-8), not dotted (claude-opus-4.8): Claude
161	#     Code only recognizes hyphenated family names — dotted ids fall back to
162	#     a legacy "[Opus 4] retired" label AND a 200k context assumption.
163	#   - "[1m]" suffix: Copilot serves opus-4-8 / sonnet-5 with a 1M context
164	#     window; the suffix makes Claude Code strip it, send the context-1m
165	#     beta header, and size HUD/compaction to 1M (otherwise it assumes 200k
166	#     and shows >100% context). Claude Code-only: raw API clients must send
167	#     the plain id — the proxy rejects a literal "...[1m]" model.
168	_copilot_default_model() {
169	  if [ -n "${COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL:-}" ]; then
170	    printf '%s' "$COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL"
171	  elif [ -f "$(_copilot_model_state)" ]; then
172	    command head -n 1 "$(_copilot_model_state)"
173	  else
174	    printf '%s' "claude-opus-4-8[1m]"
175	  fi
176	}
177	
178	# Is the proxy answering on its port?
179	_copilot_alive() {
180	  command curl -fsS --max-time 2 "$(_copilot_base)/v1/models" >/dev/null 2>&1
181	}
182	
183	# --- proxy manager --------------------------------------------------------------
184	
185	# Manage the copilot-api proxy. Subcommands: start|stop|status|restart|logs|auth.
186	# Example:
187	#   copilot-proxy start          # background-start on $COPILOT_PROXY_PORT
188	#   copilot-proxy status         # is it up? which models?
189	copilot-proxy() {
190	  if [ -n "$ZSH_VERSION" ]; then
191	    emulate -L zsh
192	    setopt localoptions pipefail
193	  else
194	    set -o pipefail
195	  fi
196	
197	  if ! command -v bunx >/dev/null 2>&1; then
198	    printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: bunx not found (needs bun via mise)." >&2
199	    return 1
200	  fi
201	
202	  local port pkg logf pidf
203	  port="$(_copilot_port)"; pkg="$(_copilot_pkg)"
204	  logf="$(_copilot_logfile)"; pidf="$(_copilot_pidfile)"
205	
206	  local action="${1:-status}"
207	  case "$action" in
208	    start)
209	      if _copilot_alive; then
210	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: already running on port $port" >&2
211	        return 0
212	      fi
213	      # First run needs `copilot-proxy auth` to store a ghu_ token; warn if absent.
214	      if [ ! -f "$HOME/.local/share/copilot-api/github_token" ]; then
215	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: not authenticated yet — run 'copilot-proxy auth' first." >&2
216	        return 1
217	      fi
218	      # Rotate the previous session's log (keep last 3) so a restart doesn't wipe
219	      # it — the start commands below truncate ($logf) via `>`. logf.1 = previous
220	      # session, logf.2/.3 = older. Debugging a transient (e.g. 403) survives.
221	      if [ -f "$logf" ]; then
222	        command rm -f "$logf.3" 2>/dev/null
223	        [ -f "$logf.2" ] && command mv -f "$logf.2" "$logf.3"
224	        [ -f "$logf.1" ] && command mv -f "$logf.1" "$logf.2"
225	        command mv -f "$logf" "$logf.1"
226	      fi
227	      # Flag sets differ per package: only the original has --rate-limit/--wait
228	      # (the fork ships no rate limiter — mitigate with COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET=1).
229	      if [ "$(_copilot_pkg_flavor)" = "original" ]; then
230	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: starting ($pkg) on port $port (rate-limit ${COPILOT_PROXY_RATE:-15}s) ..."
231	        # nohup + background; detach so it survives the shell. Log to a file.
232	        nohup bunx "$pkg" start \
233	          --port "$port" \
234	          --rate-limit "${COPILOT_PROXY_RATE:-15}" \
235	          --wait \
236	          >"$logf" 2>&1 &
237	      else
238	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: starting ($pkg) on port $port ..."
239	        nohup bunx "$pkg" start \
240	          --port "$port" \
241	          >"$logf" 2>&1 &
242	      fi
243	      printf '%s\n' "$!" >"$pidf"
244	      # Wait up to ~20s for it to answer.
245	      local i=0
246	      while [ "$i" -lt 20 ]; do
247	        if _copilot_alive; then
248	          if _copilot_shim_enabled; then
249	            _copilot_shim_start && printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: throttle shim up → $(_copilot_shim_base) (→ $(_copilot_base))"
250	          fi
251	          printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: up → $(_copilot_client_base)  (logs: copilot-proxy logs)"
252	          return 0
253	        fi
254	        sleep 1
255	        i=$((i + 1))
256	      done
257	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: did not come up in time — check 'copilot-proxy logs'." >&2
258	      return 1
259	      ;;
260	    stop)
261	      # Tear down the shim first (harmless if not running).
262	      _copilot_shim_stop
263	      # Prefer the tracked pid; fall back to a broad match.
264	      if [ -f "$pidf" ]; then
265	        local pid; pid="$(cat "$pidf" 2>/dev/null)"
266	        [ -n "$pid" ] && kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null
267	        command rm -f -- "$pidf"
268	      fi
269	      command pkill -f "copilot-api.*--port $port" 2>/dev/null
270	      sleep 1
271	      if _copilot_alive; then
272	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: still answering on $port (another instance?)" >&2
273	        return 1
274	      fi
275	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: stopped (port $port free)"
276	      ;;
277	    restart)
278	      copilot-proxy stop
279	      copilot-proxy start
280	      ;;
281	    status)
282	      if _copilot_alive; then
283	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: RUNNING on $(_copilot_base)"
284	        printf '%s\n' "  models: $(command curl -fsS --max-time 3 "$(_copilot_base)/v1/models" 2>/dev/null \
285	          | command grep -o '"id":"[^"]*"' | command sed 's/"id":"//;s/"//' | command grep -i claude | command tr '\n' ' ')"
286	        if _copilot_shim_enabled; then
287	          if _copilot_shim_alive; then
288	            printf '%s\n' "  shim:   ON, up on $(_copilot_shim_base)  → clients use this"
289	          else
290	            printf '%s\n' "  shim:   ON but DOWN (clients fall back to $(_copilot_base); try 'copilot-proxy shim on')"
291	          fi
292	        else
293	          printf '%s\n' "  shim:   off  (enable: copilot-proxy shim on)"
294	        fi
295	      else
296	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: not running on port $port  (start: copilot-proxy start)"
297	        return 1
298	      fi
299	      ;;
300	    logs)
301	      # logs [N] [gen]  — tail the fork log; gen 1..3 = a rotated prev session.
302	      # logs shim [N]    — tail the throttle shim's log instead.
303	      local _lf _n
304	      if [ "${2:-}" = "shim" ]; then
305	        _lf="$(_copilot_shim_logfile)"; _n="${3:-40}"
306	      else
307	        _lf="$logf"; _n="${2:-40}"
308	        case "${3:-}" in 1|2|3) _lf="$logf.$3" ;; esac
309	      fi
310	      if [ -f "$_lf" ]; then command tail -n "$_n" "$_lf"; else
311	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: no log file at $_lf" >&2; return 1; fi
312	      ;;
313	    auth)
314	      # One-time device login → stores a ghu_ token copilot-api can exchange.
315	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: launching copilot-api device login ..."
316	      bunx "$pkg" auth
317	      ;;
318	    whoami|usage)
319	      # Real login check: exchanges the stored token against GitHub and prints
320	      # the account / plan / quota. Fails loudly if the token is missing/expired.
321	      if [ ! -f "$HOME/.local/share/copilot-api/github_token" ]; then
322	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: not authenticated — run 'copilot-proxy auth' first." >&2
323	        return 1
324	      fi
325	      if [ "$(_copilot_pkg_flavor)" = "original" ]; then
326	        bunx "$pkg" check-usage
327	      elif _copilot_alive; then
328	        # Fork has no check-usage subcommand; its /usage endpoint serves the
329	        # GitHub quota payload (same data as the bundled /usage-viewer).
330	        if command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
331	          command curl -fsS --max-time 5 "$(_copilot_base)/usage" | jq '{
332	            plan: (.copilot_plan // .access_type_sku // "unknown"),
333	            quota_reset: (.quota_reset_date // null),
334	            quotas: ((.quota_snapshots // {}) | map_values(
335	              if type == "object" then
336	                {remaining, entitlement, percent_remaining, unlimited}
337	              else . end))
338	          }'
339	        else
340	          command curl -fsS --max-time 5 "$(_copilot_base)/usage"
341	        fi
342	      else
343	        # Proxy down → the fork's `debug` still validates auth state / paths.
344	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: not running — showing auth/debug info instead of quota." >&2
345	        bunx "$pkg" debug
346	      fi
347	      ;;
348	    shim)
349	      # shim [on|off|status] — persist the toggle, and start/stop it live if the
350	      # fork is already running.
351	      local sf; sf="$(_copilot_shim_state)"
352	      case "${2:-status}" in
353	        on)
354	          command mkdir -p "$(command dirname "$sf")"; printf 'on\n' >"$sf"
355	          if _copilot_alive; then
356	            _copilot_shim_start && printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: shim ON → $(_copilot_shim_base) (→ $(_copilot_base))"
357	          else
358	            printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: shim enabled; will start with the proxy ('copilot-proxy start')."
359	          fi
360	          printf '%s\n' "  NOTE: restart Claude Code so it picks up ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=$(_copilot_shim_base)"
361	          ;;
362	        off)
363	          printf 'off\n' >"$sf"; _copilot_shim_stop
364	          printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: shim OFF (clients use $(_copilot_base) directly)"
365	          printf '%s\n' "  NOTE: restart Claude Code to point back at $(_copilot_base)"
366	          ;;
367	        status|*)
368	          if _copilot_shim_enabled; then
369	            printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: shim ON ($(_copilot_shim_alive && echo up || echo down)) on $(_copilot_shim_base)"
370	          else
371	            printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: shim off"
372	          fi
373	          ;;
374	      esac
375	      ;;
376	    -h|--help|help)
377	      printf '%s\n' "Usage: copilot-proxy [start|stop|restart|status|logs [shim|N [gen]]|shim [on|off|status]|whoami|auth]"
378	      ;;
379	    *)
380	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-proxy: unknown action '$action' (try --help)" >&2
381	      return 1
382	      ;;
383	  esac
384	}
385	
386	# --- session wrapper (Layer 1: one-off, zero file writes) ------------------------
387	
388	# Run any command with the copilot-proxy env injected (per-process only —
389	# nothing is written to disk). Auto-starts the proxy when it isn't answering.
390	# GOTCHA (verified 2026-07): current Claude Code lets the settings.local.json
391	# env block beat inherited shell env, so this does NOT win inside a project
392	# where `copilot-here on` pins something else — `copilot-here off` first.
393	# Example:
394	#   copilot-run claude                      # raw Claude Code on the proxy
395	#   copilot-run specstory run claude        # specstory-wrapped session
396	copilot-run() {
397	  if [ "$#" -eq 0 ]; then
398	    printf '%s\n' "Usage: copilot-run <cmd> [args...]   (injects ANTHROPIC_* proxy env)" >&2
399	    return 1
400	  fi
401	  if ! _copilot_alive; then
402	    copilot-proxy start || return 1
403	  fi
404	  # If the shim is enabled but not up (e.g. toggled on after the proxy started),
405	  # bring it up now so ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL below resolves to it.
406	  if _copilot_shim_enabled && ! _copilot_shim_alive; then _copilot_shim_start; fi
407	  local model; model="$(_copilot_default_model)"
408	  # Opt-in quota savers (COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET=1): prepended as NAME=VALUE args
409	  # to `env`. Off by default — they degrade the Claude Code UX a little.
410	  if [ "${COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET:-0}" = "1" ]; then
411	    set -- \
412	      CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER="0" \
413	      CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_PROMPT_SUGGESTION="false" \
414	      CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY="0" \
415	      DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS="1" \
416	      "$@"
417	  fi
418	  # `command env` (not bare var-prefix) so the vars are strictly per-process:
419	  # POSIX var-prefix on a *function* call would leak into the current shell.
420	  command env \
421	    ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL="$(_copilot_client_base)" \
422	    ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN="dummy" \
423	    ANTHROPIC_MODEL="$model" \
424	    ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL="$model" \
425	    ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL="claude-sonnet-5[1m]" \
426	    ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL="claude-haiku-4-5" \
427	    ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL="claude-haiku-4-5" \
428	    CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC="1" \
429	    "$@"
430	}
431	
432	# One-off Claude Code session backed by the Copilot proxy. Nothing on disk
433	# changes — revert is just running plain `claude` next time.
434	# Wraps in `specstory run` when specstory is installed (markdown auto-save,
435	# same convention as scode/svibe); opt out with --no-specstory. Extra args go
436	# to the claude CLI (via specstory's -c "custom command" passthrough).
437	# Example:
438	#   claude-copilot                 # specstory run claude (proxy env)
439	#   claude-copilot -c              # continue last session
440	#   claude-copilot --no-specstory  # raw claude, no markdown auto-save
441	claude-copilot() {
442	  local ss="auto"
443	  case "${1:-}" in
444	    --no-specstory) ss="never"; shift ;;
445	    --specstory)    shift ;;
446	    -h|--help)
447	      printf '%s\n' "Usage: claude-copilot [--no-specstory] [claude args...]"
448	      printf '%s\n' "  One-off Claude Code session on the Copilot proxy (no file writes)."
449	      printf '%s\n' "  Sticky per-project instead: copilot-here on"
450	      return 0 ;;
451	  esac
452	  if [ "$ss" = "auto" ] && command -v specstory >/dev/null 2>&1; then
453	    if [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; then
454	      copilot-run specstory run claude -c "claude $*"
455	    else
456	      copilot-run specstory run claude
457	    fi
458	  else
459	    copilot-run claude "$@"
460	  fi
461	}
462	
463	# --- per-project toggle (Layer 2: sticky, gitignored settings.local.json) --------
464	
465	# Env keys we own in .claude/settings.local.json (kept in one place so `off`
466	# removes exactly what `on` added — including the COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET extras,
467	# regardless of the knob's value at `off` time).
468	_copilot_here_keys='["ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL","ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN","ANTHROPIC_MODEL","ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL","ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL","ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL","ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL","CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC","CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER","CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_PROMPT_SUGGESTION","CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY","DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS"]'
469	
470	# Pin THIS project to the Copilot proxy via ./.claude/settings.local.json —
471	# the gitignored local layer that overrides the committed .claude/settings.json
472	# (so claude-plans-here's plansDirectory stays untouched). Plain `claude` then
473	# uses the proxy here until `copilot-here off`.
474	# Example:
475	#   copilot-here on        # pin project to the proxy
476	#   copilot-here status    # pinned? which model?
477	#   copilot-here off       # unpin (removes only our env keys)
478	copilot-here() {
479	  if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
480	    printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: jq is required" >&2; return 1
481	  fi
482	
483	  local settings=".claude/settings.local.json"
484	  local action="${1:-status}"
485	  case "$action" in
486	    on)
487	      command mkdir -p .claude
488	      local base='{}'
489	      [ -f "$settings" ] && base="$(command cat "$settings")"
490	      [ -n "$base" ] || base='{}'
491	      local model; model="$(_copilot_default_model)"
492	      local quiet="${COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET:-0}"
493	      local tmp; tmp="$(command mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/copilot-here.XXXXXX")" || return 1
494	      if printf '%s' "$base" | jq \
495	          --arg base_url "$(_copilot_pinned_base)" --arg model "$model" --arg quiet "$quiet" '
496	          .env = ((.env // {}) + {
497	            ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL: $base_url,
498	            ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN: "dummy",
499	            ANTHROPIC_MODEL: $model,
500	            ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL: $model,
501	            ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL: "claude-sonnet-5[1m]",
502	            ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL: "claude-haiku-4-5",
503	            ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL: "claude-haiku-4-5",
504	            CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC: "1"
505	          } + (if $quiet == "1" then {
506	            CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER: "0",
507	            CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_PROMPT_SUGGESTION: "false",
508	            CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY: "0",
509	            DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS: "1"
510	          } else {} end))' >"$tmp"; then
511	        command mv -- "$tmp" "$settings"
512	      else
513	        command rm -f -- "$tmp"
514	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: jq failed; $settings unchanged" >&2
515	        return 1
516	      fi
517	      # Claude Code only auto-gitignores settings.local.json when IT creates the
518	      # file — since we created it, belt-and-braces via .git/info/exclude (a
519	      # per-clone, never-committed ignore, so the proxy toggle leaves nothing to
520	      # commit — unlike Claude Code's own root-.gitignore entry).
521	      if command git rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then
522	        if ! command git check-ignore -q "$settings" 2>/dev/null; then
523	          local exclude; exclude="$(command git rev-parse --git-dir)/info/exclude"
524	          # MUST be the un-anchored **/ form, NOT a bare .claude/settings.local.json:
525	          # a pattern with an internal slash is anchored to the repo ROOT, so it
526	          # would not ignore the file when THIS project lives in a subdirectory of
527	          # a larger repo (e.g. skills/foo/.claude/…). **/ matches at any depth —
528	          # one idempotent line covers root + every nested project.
529	          printf '%s\n' "**/.claude/settings.local.json" >>"$exclude"
530	        fi
531	      fi
532	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: ON — $settings pins Claude Code to $(_copilot_base) (model: $model)"
533	      printf '%s\n' "  plain \`claude\` in this project now uses the proxy (restart any running session)"
534	      _copilot_alive || printf '%s\n' "  ⚠ proxy not running — start it with: copilot-proxy start"
535	      ;;
536	    off)
537	      if [ ! -f "$settings" ]; then
538	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: already off (no $settings)"
539	        return 0
540	      fi
541	      local tmp; tmp="$(command mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/copilot-here.XXXXXX")" || return 1
542	      # Remove only OUR env keys; drop .env when it empties; keep other content.
543	      if jq --argjson keys "$_copilot_here_keys" '
544	          .env = ((.env // {}) | with_entries(select(.key as $k | ($keys | index($k)) == null)))
545	          | if .env == {} then del(.env) else . end' \
546	          "$settings" >"$tmp"; then
547	        if [ "$(jq -r 'if . == {} then "empty" else "kept" end' "$tmp")" = "empty" ]; then
548	          command rm -f -- "$tmp" "$settings"
549	          printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: OFF — removed $settings (it held only proxy config)"
550	        else
551	          command mv -- "$tmp" "$settings"
552	          printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: OFF — proxy env removed from $settings (other content kept)"
553	        fi
554	        printf '%s\n' "  plain \`claude\` is back on the real Anthropic backend (restart any running session)"
555	      else
556	        command rm -f -- "$tmp"
557	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: jq failed; $settings unchanged" >&2
558	        return 1
559	      fi
560	      ;;
561	    status)
562	      if [ -f "$settings" ] && [ "$(jq -r '.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL // empty' "$settings" 2>/dev/null)" != "" ]; then
563	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: ON  (base: $(jq -r '.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL' "$settings"), model: $(jq -r '.env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL // "(unset)"' "$settings"))"
564	        _copilot_alive || printf '%s\n' "  ⚠ proxy not running — start it with: copilot-proxy start"
565	      else
566	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: off  (enable: copilot-here on; one-off: claude-copilot)"
567	        return 1
568	      fi
569	      ;;
570	    -h|--help|help)
571	      printf '%s\n' "Usage: copilot-here [on|off|status]   (toggles ./.claude/settings.local.json)"
572	      ;;
573	    *)
574	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-here: unknown action '$action' (try --help)" >&2
575	      return 1
576	      ;;
577	  esac
578	}
579	
580	# --- one-shot pinned session (Layer 2, auto-reverted) ---------------------------
581	
582	# Combine copilot-here (settings.local.json pin — beats inherited env, see Gotchas)
583	# with claude-copilot's ephemerality: pin THIS project, run ONE specstory-wrapped
584	# session, then unpin — even on Ctrl-C / kill. The proxy must already be up (we only
585	# notify; start it with `copilot-proxy start`). If copilot-here is already ON here,
586	# the existing pin is left untouched on exit (safe to run inside a pinned project).
587	# Example:
588	#   claude-copilot-once                 # pin, run, auto-unpin
589	#   claude-copilot-once -c              # continue last session
590	#   claude-copilot-once --no-specstory  # raw claude, no markdown auto-save
591	claude-copilot-once() {
592	  case "${1:-}" in
593	    -h|--help)
594	      printf '%s\n' "Usage: claude-copilot-once [--no-specstory] [claude args...]"
595	      printf '%s\n' "  Pin THIS project to the proxy (copilot-here on), run one Claude Code"
596	      printf '%s\n' "  session, then auto-unpin (copilot-here off) — even on Ctrl-C."
597	      printf '%s\n' "  Needs the proxy already running:  copilot-proxy start"
598	      return 0 ;;
599	  esac
600	
601	  # 1. Proxy must already be answering — notify only, never auto-start.
602	  if ! _copilot_alive; then
603	    printf '%s\n' "claude-copilot-once: proxy not reachable on port $(_copilot_port)." >&2
604	    printf '%s\n' "  start it first:  copilot-proxy start" >&2
605	    return 1
606	  fi
607	  # copilot-here needs jq — fail early with a clear message.
608	  if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
609	    printf '%s\n' "claude-copilot-once: jq is required (used by copilot-here)" >&2
610	    return 1
611	  fi
612	
613	  # 2. Don't clobber an existing pin: only turn OFF what we turn ON.
614	  local _cco_was_on=0
615	  if [ -f ".claude/settings.local.json" ] \
616	     && [ "$(jq -r '.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL // empty' ".claude/settings.local.json" 2>/dev/null)" != "" ]; then
617	    _cco_was_on=1
618	  fi
619	
620	  if [ "$_cco_was_on" = "0" ]; then
621	    copilot-here on || return 1
622	    # Auto-unpin even on Ctrl-C / kill. Mirror tmux_status_run's INT/TERM/HUP
623	    # trap + explicit normal-path cleanup; a bare function-scope EXIT trap would
624	    # fire on the wrong event when bash sources this file.
625	    trap '_copilot_once_trap' INT TERM HUP
626	  else
627	    printf '%s\n' "claude-copilot-once: copilot-here already ON here — leaving the pin in place on exit."
628	  fi
629	
630	  # 3. One session — specstory-wrapped + arg passthrough (reuses claude-copilot).
631	  claude-copilot "$@"
632	  local _rc=$?
633	
634	  # 4. Revert only what we enabled.
635	  if [ "$_cco_was_on" = "0" ]; then
636	    trap - INT TERM HUP
637	    copilot-here off
638	  fi
639	
640	  # 5. We never auto-stop the proxy — remind how.
641	  printf '%s\n' "claude-copilot-once: session ended. Proxy still running on $(_copilot_base)."
642	  printf '%s\n' "  stop it when done:  copilot-proxy stop"
643	  return $_rc
644	}
645	
646	# Internal: INT/TERM/HUP handler — unpin, clear trap, re-raise INT so the
647	# interactive shell sees correct exit semantics (mirrors _tmux_status_run_trap
648	# in dot_config/shell/60_tmux_status.sh).
649	_copilot_once_trap() {
650	  copilot-here off >/dev/null 2>&1
651	  trap - INT TERM HUP
652	  printf '\n%s\n' "claude-copilot-once: interrupted — unpinned (copilot-here off). Proxy still up." >&2
653	  kill -INT $$ 2>/dev/null
654	}
655	
656	# --- model switcher -------------------------------------------------------------
657	
658	# Switch which Copilot model is pinned. Claude Code's own /model picker sends
659	# dated Anthropic ids the Copilot backend rejects (model_not_supported), so pin
660	# here. Use the HYPHENATED ids the proxy lists (claude-opus-4-8); an optional
661	# "[1m]" suffix tells Claude Code the model has a 1M context window (see
662	# _copilot_default_model) — it is stripped before validating against the proxy.
663	#
664	# Write target (never the committed .claude/settings.json):
665	#   - copilot-here is ON in this project → ./.claude/settings.local.json
666	#   - otherwise → global state file (~/.local/state/copilot-proxy/model), which
667	#     claude-copilot / copilot-run / the next `copilot-here on` pick up.
668	# Example:
669	#   copilot-model opus-4-8         # fuzzy → claude-opus-4-8 (opus-4.8 works too)
670	#   copilot-model 'opus-4-8[1m]'   # same + 1M-context hint for Claude Code
671	#   copilot-model -l               # list available (live from proxy)
672	#   copilot-model -c               # print current (+ where it came from)
673	copilot-model() {
674	  if [ -n "$ZSH_VERSION" ]; then
675	    emulate -L zsh
676	    setopt localoptions pipefail
677	  else
678	    set -o pipefail
679	  fi
680	
681	  local settings=".claude/settings.local.json"
682	  local statef; statef="$(_copilot_model_state)"
683	
684	  # copilot-here mode? (local settings file carries our proxy env)
685	  local target="state"
686	  if [ -f "$settings" ] && command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 \
687	     && [ "$(jq -r '.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL // empty' "$settings" 2>/dev/null)" != "" ]; then
688	    target="local"
689	  fi
690	
691	  # List available model ids (live proxy, else a static Claude fallback).
692	  _copilot_model_list() {
693	    local json
694	    if json="$(command curl -fsS --max-time 3 "$(_copilot_base)/v1/models" 2>/dev/null)"; then
695	      printf '%s\n' "$json" | command grep -o '"id":"[^"]*"' | command sed 's/"id":"//;s/"//' | command sort
696	    else
697	      printf '%s\n' \
698	        claude-opus-4-8 claude-opus-4-7 claude-opus-4-6 claude-opus-4-5 \
699	        claude-sonnet-5 claude-sonnet-4-6 claude-sonnet-4-5 claude-haiku-4-5
700	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-model: proxy not reachable — showing fallback list" >&2
701	    fi
702	  }
703	
704	  # Current pinned model, from whichever layer is active.
705	  _copilot_model_current() {
706	    if [ "$target" = "local" ]; then
707	      jq -r '.env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL // "(unset)"' "$settings"
708	    else
709	      _copilot_default_model
710	    fi
711	  }
712	
713	  local arg="${1:-}"
714	  case "$arg" in
715	    -l|--list) _copilot_model_list; return 0 ;;
716	    -c|--current)
717	      if [ "$target" = "local" ]; then
718	        printf '%s  (project: %s)\n' "$(_copilot_model_current)" "$settings"
719	      else
720	        printf '%s  (global: %s)\n' "$(_copilot_model_current)" "$statef"
721	      fi
722	      return 0 ;;
723	    -h|--help)
724	      printf '%s\n' "Usage: copilot-model [<model-id>|-l|-c]"
725	      printf '%s\n' "  Writes ./.claude/settings.local.json when copilot-here is on,"
726	      printf '%s\n' "  else the global state file used by claude-copilot / copilot-run."
727	      return 0 ;;
728	  esac
729	
730	  if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
731	    printf '%s\n' "copilot-model: jq is required" >&2; return 1
732	  fi
733	
734	  local models want resolved
735	  models="$(_copilot_model_list 2>/dev/null)"
736	
737	  # No arg + fzf available → interactive pick.
738	  if [ -z "$arg" ]; then
739	    if ! command -v fzf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
740	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-model: pass a model id (fzf not found). Try: copilot-model -l" >&2
741	      return 1
742	    fi
743	    local cur; cur="$(_copilot_model_current)"
744	    want="$(printf '%s\n' "$models" | command fzf --prompt="model> " --height=40% --reverse \
745	      --header="current: $cur")" || { printf '%s\n' "cancelled"; return 0; }
746	    [ -n "$want" ] || { printf '%s\n' "cancelled"; return 0; }
747	    resolved="$want"
748	  else
749	    # "[1m]" is a Claude Code-only 1M-context hint, not a real proxy id:
750	    # strip it for validation, re-append on the resolved id.
751	    local suffix="" base="$arg"
752	    case "$arg" in
753	      *"[1m]") suffix="[1m]"; base="${arg%\[1m\]}" ;;
754	    esac
755	    # Proxy ids are hyphenated (claude-opus-4-8) but muscle memory says
756	    # opus-4.8 — normalize dots to hyphens and accept both.
757	    local norm; norm="$(printf '%s' "$base" | command tr '.' '-')"
758	    # Resolve: exact, else claude-<arg>, else unique substring.
759	    local cand; resolved=""
760	    for cand in "$base" "claude-$base" "$norm" "claude-$norm"; do
761	      if printf '%s\n' "$models" | command grep -qxF "$cand"; then
762	        resolved="$cand"
763	        break
764	      fi
765	    done
766	    if [ -z "$resolved" ]; then
767	      local hits count
768	      hits="$(printf '%s\n' "$models" | command grep -F "$norm" || true)"
769	      count="$(printf '%s\n' "$hits" | command grep -c . )"
770	      if [ "$count" = "1" ] && [ -n "$hits" ]; then
771	        resolved="$hits"
772	      else
773	        printf '%s\n' "copilot-model: '$arg' did not match a unique model. Try: copilot-model -l" >&2
774	        return 1
775	      fi
776	    fi
777	    resolved="$resolved$suffix"
778	  fi
779	
780	  local old; old="$(_copilot_model_current)"
781	  if [ "$old" = "$resolved" ]; then
782	    printf '%s\n' "copilot-model: already using $resolved (no change)"
783	    return 0
784	  fi
785	
786	  if [ "$target" = "local" ]; then
787	    local tmp; tmp="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/copilot-model.XXXXXX")" || return 1
788	    if jq --arg m "$resolved" \
789	         '.env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL = $m | .env.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL = $m' \
790	         "$settings" >"$tmp"; then
791	      command mv -- "$tmp" "$settings"
792	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-model: $old -> $resolved  (project: $settings)"
793	      printf '%s\n' "  ⟳ restart Claude Code to apply (exit, then: claude -c)"
794	    else
795	      command rm -f -- "$tmp"
796	      printf '%s\n' "copilot-model: failed to update $settings" >&2
797	      return 1
798	    fi
799	  else
800	    command mkdir -p "$(command dirname "$statef")"
801	    printf '%s\n' "$resolved" >"$statef"
802	    printf '%s\n' "copilot-model: $old -> $resolved  (global: $statef)"
803	    printf '%s\n' "  applies to the next claude-copilot / copilot-run / copilot-here on"
804	  fi
805	}
806
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 11:06:06Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/docs/tools/llm.md`

```text
1	# Local LLM Tools
2	
3	Optional role (`installLlmTools`). Install via `chezmoi init --force` and enable the prompt.
4	
5	## Related Notes
6	
7	- [OpenCode](opencode.md): local-only OpenCode config notes, known title-generation regression, and current workaround.
8	
9	## Tools
10	
11	| Tool | Binary | Purpose | Install path |
12	|------|--------|---------|--------------|
13	| [Ollama](https://ollama.com/) | `ollama` | Local LLM runtime and API server | macOS: Homebrew formula or `ollama-app` via Brewfile; Linux: official installer |
14	| [LiteLLM](https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm) | `litellm` | Local proxy / unified API for LLM backends | `uv tool install litellm[proxy] --with httpx[socks]` |
15	| [llmfit](https://github.com/AlexsJones/llmfit) | `llmfit` | Recommend models that fit your hardware | macOS: Homebrew formula; Linux: official local installer |
16	| [models](https://github.com/arimxyer/models) | `models` | Browse models, benchmarks, coding agents, provider status | macOS/Linuxbrew: Homebrew formula; Linux fallback: `cargo install modelsdev` |
17	
18	## Platform Notes
19	
20	- **macOS, `installBrewApps=false`**: ansible installs `ollama`, `llmfit`, and `models` via Homebrew formulas.
21	- **macOS, `installBrewApps=true`**: ansible still installs `llmfit` and `models`, but `ollama` is deferred to `~/.config/homebrew/Brewfile.darwin` as `ollama-app`.
22	- **Linux with sudo**: ansible installs `litellm`, `llmfit`, `models`, and `ollama`.
23	- **Linux with `noRoot=true`**: ansible installs `litellm`, `llmfit`, and `models`, and explicitly skips `ollama`.
24	
25	## macOS GUI vs CLI
26	
27	- `ollama-app` is preferred when you already opted into GUI apps with `installBrewApps=true`.
28	- `brew install ollama` is used when you want the CLI/runtime without the GUI app.
29	- The macOS app also exposes the `ollama` CLI from inside the app bundle.
30	
31	## Common Usage
32	
33	```bash
34	# Start the Ollama API server
35	ollama serve
36	
37	# Run the LiteLLM proxy
38	litellm --help
39	
40	# Check which models fit your hardware
41	llmfit
42	
43	# Browse models and benchmarks
44	models
45	```
46	
47	## noRoot Reminder
48	
49	`noRoot=true` skips `ollama` because the official Linux install path sets up system locations and a systemd service. The other tools remain user-level installs.
50
```
</details></tool-use>

############ executable_fleet (first 60 lines) ############
#!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --quiet --script
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.11"
# dependencies = [
#   "asyncssh>=2.18",
#   "tyro>=0.9",
#   "rich>=13.9",
# ]
# ///
"""fleet — umbrella CLI for the dotfiles fleet.

Top-level layout (2026-05-13):

  fleet chezmoi <action>         Chezmoi-specific deploy ops (apply / status /
                                 diff / tail / kill / compact). Backed by
                                 scripts/fleet/apply.py.
  fleet tmux [...]               Cross-host tmux session summary
  fleet info [...]               Cross-host system status snapshot
  fleet pueue [...]              Cross-host pueue queue summary
  fleet exec [OPTS] -- CMD [...]  Cross-host ad-hoc command runner
  fleet hosts [NAME|--list*]     Interactive picker (via `tv fleet-hosts`)
                                 for SSH'ing into a fleet-configured host.
                                 Backed by scripts/fleet/hosts.py.
  fleet edit                     $EDITOR ~/.config/fleet/machines.toml
                                 (fleet-meta — used by every subcommand)

The `fleet chezmoi <action>` namespace was introduced to free the top
level for generic primitives. `apply` / `status` / `diff` / etc. are NO
LONGER top-level subcommands — they only exist under `chezmoi`. The
`just fleet-*` recipes still call scripts/fleet/apply.py directly with
its underlying flags (`--readiness`, `--status`, `--kill-orphans`,
`--compact`, etc.) and continue to work unchanged.

Discovers the chezmoi source dir at runtime via `chezmoi source-path` (falls
back to ~/.local/share/chezmoi), so the binary works from any cwd.

Deployed by chezmoi as ~/.dotfiles/bin/fleet. See docs/this_repo/fleet-apply.md.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

USAGE = """\
Usage: fleet <subcommand> [args...]

Chezmoi-specific deploy ops (under `chezmoi` namespace):
  chezmoi apply [--hosts ...] [--dry-run] [--force] [...]
                          Run chezmoi update --init across the fleet.
  chezmoi status          Pre-flight readiness probe (predicts apply outcome).
  chezmoi status --quick  Skip remote `git fetch` (faster).
  chezmoi status --live   Process-liveness probe (use during/after apply).
  chezmoi status --live --watch N  Poll every N seconds.
  chezmoi diff HOST [args...]
                          Shortcut: chezmoi apply --dry-run --hosts HOST --serial.
  chezmoi tail HOST[:RUN_ID]
                          Live-tail a host's fleet-apply log.

############ executable_mlf (first 60 lines) ############
#!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --quiet --script
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.11"
# dependencies = [
#   "mlflow>=3.4",
#   "tyro>=0.9",
#   "rich>=13.9",
#   "plotext>=5.2",
#   "textual>=5.0",
# ]
# ///
"""mlf — umbrella CLI for MLflow operations.

Bare `mlf` (no args) launches the interactive Textual TUI dashboard.
Subcommands below bypass it for scripted automation.

Subcommands:
  tui                            Launch the interactive dashboard (default)
  tv [...]                       Launch `tv mlflow` (the fuzzy picker channel)
  open <id>                      Open the experiment / run / model page in browser
  copy <id>                      Copy an id to the clipboard
  plot <run_id> [metric...]      Terminal line plot of metric history (scripts/mlf/plot.py)
  list <kind> [...]              Tabular dump of experiments / runs / models (scripts/mlf/list.py)
  download <name>[@version|alias] Download a registered model's artifacts (scripts/mlf/download.py)

Backend is read from $MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI (file:./mlruns | sqlite:///path | http(s)://host:port).
`open` requires an http(s):// URI; other subcommands work against all three.

Mirrors the structure of `fleet` (see dot_dotfiles/bin/executable_fleet).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

USAGE = """\
Usage: mlf [<subcommand>] [args...]

Bare `mlf` (or `mlf tui`) launches the interactive Textual dashboard.
Subcommands bypass the TUI for scripted use.

Subcommands:
  tui                                Launch the interactive dashboard
                                     (equivalent to bare `mlf`)

  tv [args...]                       Open the `tv mlflow` fuzzy picker
                                     (passes argv + env through, including
                                      MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI / MLFLOW_EXPERIMENT_ID)

  open <id>                          Open in browser; auto-detects entity kind:
                                       32-hex  -> /#/experiments/<exp>/runs/<id>
                                       digits  -> /#/experiments/<id>
                                       else    -> /#/models/<name>
                                     Requires MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI to be http(s)://.

  copy <id>                          Copy <id> to system clipboard
                                     (pbcopy / wl-copy / xclip / OSC52 fallback).

############ executable_x (first 60 lines) ############
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

usage() {
    printf '%s\n' \
        "Usage:" \
        "  x copy [--force] [FILE]" \
        "  x copy-file [--force] PATH..." \
        "  x paste" \
        "  x open TARGET" \
        "  x help" \
        "" \
        "Examples:" \
        "  printf \"hello\" | x copy" \
        "  x copy ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub" \
        "  x copy-file ~/Downloads/report.pdf" \
        "  x paste" \
        "  x open https://example.com"
}

err() {
    printf 'x: %s\n' "$*" >&2
}

is_wsl() {
    local version
    [[ -r /proc/version ]] || return 1
    version="$(< /proc/version)"
    [[ "$version" == *[Mm]icrosoft* || "$version" == *[Ww][Ss][Ll]* ]]
}

is_macos() {
    [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Darwin" ]]
}

clipboard_backend_help() {
    printf '%s\n' \
        "x: no clipboard backend found." \
        "x: install one of:" \
        "x:   - macOS: pbcopy/pbpaste (built-in)" \
        "x:   - Linux Wayland: wl-clipboard (wl-copy/wl-paste)" \
        "x:   - Linux X11: xclip or xsel" \
        "x:   - WSL: clip.exe / powershell.exe" \
        "x:   - SSH/headless: OSC 52 (needs a TTY and a terminal that supports it)" >&2
}

file_clipboard_backend_help() {
    printf '%s\n' \
        "x: no file clipboard backend found." \
        "x: install/use one of:" \
        "x:   - macOS: osascript (built-in)" \
        "x:   - Linux Wayland: wl-clipboard (wl-copy)" \
        "x:   - Linux X11: xclip" \
        "x: file-object copy is not available over SSH/OSC 52; use 'x copy FILE' for contents or 'realpath FILE | x copy' for a path." >&2
}

# OSC 52 clipboard copy: ships stdin bytes through a terminal escape sequence.
# Works over SSH with no X/Wayland as long as the terminal (or tmux with
# set-clipboard on) forwards OSC 52. Writes to /dev/tty so it is not captured
# by an outer pipeline.

############ executable_yth (first 60 lines) ############
#!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --quiet --script
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.11"
# dependencies = [
#   "yt-dlp>=2025.1",
#   "tyro>=0.9",
#   "rich>=13.9",
#   "platformdirs>=4",
# ]
# ///
"""yth — search your own YouTube watch history from the terminal.

Bare `yth` (no args) launches the `tv yth` fuzzy picker over your history.
Subcommands below bypass it for scripted / one-shot use.

Subcommands:
  import-takeout <file>          Backfill history from a Google Takeout
                                 watch-history.json (primary, cookie-free).
  sync                           Incremental sync of recent history via
                                 yt-dlp :ythistory (needs login cookies).
  enrich [--limit N | --all]     Fill missing description/duration/upload_date
                                 for videos (yt-dlp; cookie-free for public).
  fetch-subs <id>… | --recent N  Download + index subtitle text (auto+manual).
             | --all
  search <query> [--subs]        FTS5 search over title/channel/description
         [--json] [--limit N]    (add --subs to also match caption text).
  list [--tsv] [--limit N]       All history, newest first (--tsv feeds tv).
  show <id> [--json]             Detail for one video (DB-only, no network).
  open <id> | copy <id>          Open in browser / copy URL to clipboard.
  play <id>                      mpv if configured+installed, else browser.

Data lives in a local sqlite DB (platformdirs user_data_dir/yth/history.db).
Cookie source + subtitle langs are configured in user_config_dir/yth/config.toml.

Mirrors the structure of `mlf` (see dot_dotfiles/bin/executable_mlf) and pairs
with the `tv yth` channel (dot_config/television/cable/yth.toml).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

USAGE = """\
Usage: yth [<subcommand>] [args...]

Bare `yth` opens the `tv yth` fuzzy picker. Subcommands bypass it.

  import-takeout <file>              Import a Google Takeout watch-history.json.
  sync                               Incremental :ythistory sync (needs cookies).
  enrich [--limit N | --all]         Fetch per-video metadata (public: no cookies).
  fetch-subs <id>... | --recent N    Download + index subtitle text.
             | --all
  search <query> [--subs] [--json]   FTS5 search (title/channel/description;
         [--limit N] [--raw]         --subs also matches captions).
  list [--tsv] [--limit N]           List history, newest first (--tsv for tv).
  show <id> [--json]                 One-video detail (DB-only).
  open <id> | copy <id> | play <id>  Open / copy URL / play.
```
</details></tool-use>

############ pqsum (first 50) ############
#!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --quiet --script
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.11"
# dependencies = [
#   "rich>=13.9",
# ]
# ///
"""pqsum — pueue queue summary (text + AI + cleanup verdicts).

Subcommands (positional, default `text`):
  text                Tabular summary (overall + status breakdown + per-group).
  ai                  AI summary with per-group cleanability + per-task recovery hints.
  json                Raw parsed JSON to stdout (machine-readable).

AI flags:
  --report            Emit a markdown report (Summary / Cleanup Candidates /
                      Failures Needing Action / Raw status) instead of Rich blocks.
  --out PATH          Write the report to PATH (default: stdout).
  --clean             Render report, then for each `safe-to-clean` group prompt y/N
                      and run its cleanup_command. `review`/`keep` are NEVER touched.
  --yes               Skip y/N prompts (still only acts on `safe-to-clean`).
  --multi-host        Tell the LLM the input describes multiple hosts (used by `fleet pueue --ai`).
  --stdin-json        Read pre-merged pueue JSON from stdin (fleet feeds this).
                      Schema: list of host snapshots, each with the parsed shape this script emits.
  --deep              Include the last ~20 lines of each Failed task's log in the prompt
                      (more recovery context, more tokens).
  -r, --refresh       Bypass the prompt-hash cache.
  --no-cache          Don't read or write the cache.
  --dry-run           Print the constructed prompt; no AI call.

Shared / text flags:
  -g GROUP, --group GROUP
                      Restrict to one pueue group.

Env (mirrors dot_config/shell/04_ai_agents.sh SSOT):
  AICAP_AGENT, AICAP_{CLAUDE,OPENCODE,CODEX,CURSOR}_MODEL, AICAP_HTTP_*,
  AICAP_AGENT_PRIORITY, PQSUM_MIN_REFRESH_INTERVAL (sec, default 120),
  PQSUM_TIMEOUT (sec, default 60).

Cache:
  $XDG_CACHE_HOME/pqsum/<hostname>-<mode>.json
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import hashlib
import json
import os
import re
import shutil

############ mi-router (first 40) ############
#!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --quiet --script
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.11"
# dependencies = [
#   "tyro>=0.8",
#   "httpx>=0.27",
#   "rich>=13",
#   "platformdirs>=4",
# ]
# ///
"""Read-only CLI for inspecting MiWiFi (Xiaomi) routers.

Tested against Xiaomi AX9000 (RA70), MiWiFi firmware 1.0.168.

Auth uses MiWiFi's SHA1 challenge handshake. Credentials come from (in order):
  1. CLI flags (--host / --username / --password)
  2. ~/.config/mi-router/config.toml
  3. env vars (MI_ROUTER_HOST / MI_ROUTER_USER / MI_ROUTER_PASS)
  4. interactive getpass prompt (TTY only; offers to save to config)

Sister tool to ~/bin/sms (Huawei HiLink router SMS reader).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import getpass
import hashlib
import json
import os
import random
import re
import sys
import time
import tomllib
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Union

import httpx
import tyro
from platformdirs import user_config_dir

############ ping-monitor (first 30) ############
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# ping-monitor — record ping over time and flag latency spikes.
#
# Normal Wi-Fi gaming shows a low, steady baseline with occasional huge
# spikes. Pinging your own gateway isolates whether those spikes are local
# (Wi-Fi / router) vs. upstream: if the GATEWAY already spikes, the problem
# is the local wireless medium, not the ISP / game server. See
# docs/playbooks/wifi-latency-spikes.md.
#
# Usage:
#   ping-monitor [TARGET] [SPIKE_MS] [INTERVAL_S]
#   ping-monitor -g | --gateway [SPIKE_MS] [INTERVAL_S]
#
# Examples:
#   ping-monitor                 # ping default gateway, spike >100ms
#   ping-monitor -g 10           # gateway, flag anything >10ms (local test)
#   ping-monitor 8.8.8.8 150 0.5 # upstream, >150ms spike, every 0.5s
#
# Ctrl-C prints a summary (min/avg/max, p50/p95/p99, spike count, loss).
# A full log is written to ~/ping-monitor-YYYYmmdd-HHMMSS.log.

set -u

is_macos() { [[ "$(uname -s)" == Darwin ]]; }

default_gateway() {
    if is_macos; then
        route -n get default 2>/dev/null | awk '/gateway:/{print $2; exit}'
    else
```
