<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"os"
	"strings"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
	"golang.org/x/term"

	"translate/internal/config"
	"translate/internal/engine"
	"translate/internal/lang"
)

var (
	flagTo       string
	flagFrom     string
	flagModel    string
	flagProvider string
	flagEngine   string
	flagTier     string
)

// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
	root := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "translate [text...]",
		Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
		Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
			"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
			"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
			"  translate                           interactive TUI",
		SilenceUsage:  true,
		SilenceErrors: true,
		RunE:          runRoot,
	}
	f := root.PersistentFlags()
	f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
	f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
	f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
	f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
	f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
	f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")

	root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
	root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd())
	return root
}

// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
	return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
}

// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
func overrides() config.Overrides {
	return config.Overrides{
		Source:   flagFrom,
		Target:   flagTo,
		Engine:   flagEngine,
		Provider: flagProvider,
		Model:    flagModel,
		Tier:     flagTier,
	}
}

func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
	ctx := cmd.Context()

	cfg, created, err := config.Load()
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if created {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
	}

	res := cfg.Resolve(overrides())
	src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)

	if res.Provider == nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
	}
	eng := engine.NewLLM(engine.LLMConfig{
		Name:      res.Provider.Name,
		BaseURL:   res.Provider.BaseURL,
		Model:     res.Model,
		APIKeyEnv: res.Provider.APIKeyEnv,
	})

	switch {
	case len(args) > 0:
		return oneShot(ctx, eng, strings.Join(args, " "), src, tgt)

	case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
		b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
		if text == "" {
			return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
		}
		return oneShot(ctx, eng, text, src, tgt)

	default:
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "interactive TUI arrives in a later slice — pass text as arguments or pipe stdin for now.")
		return nil
	}
}

// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
	sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
	tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
	if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
	}
	if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
	}
	return sm.Code, tm.Code
}

// oneShot translates text once and prints the result. ANSI/streaming refinements
// arrive in later slices; for now it prints the plain translation.
func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string) error {
	req := engine.Request{
		Text:   text,
		Source: source,
		Target: target,
		Mode:   engine.ModeTranslate,
	}
	ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	res, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	fmt.Println(res.Translation)
	return nil
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package cmd

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"

	"translate/internal/config"
)

func newConfigCmd() *cobra.Command {
	c := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "config",
		Short: "Show or locate the configuration",
	}
	c.AddCommand(
		&cobra.Command{
			Use:   "path",
			Short: "Print the config file path",
			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
				fmt.Println(config.Path())
				return nil
			},
		},
		&cobra.Command{
			Use:   "show",
			Short: "Print the effective configuration as TOML",
			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
				cfg, _, err := config.Load()
				if err != nil {
					return err
				}
				b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
				if err != nil {
					return err
				}
				_, err = os.Stdout.Write(b)
				return err
			},
		},
	)
	return c
}

```

```go
package cmd

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"

	"translate/internal/lang"
)

func newLangCmd() *cobra.Command {
	c := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "lang",
		Short: "Language-code utilities",
	}
	c.AddCommand(&cobra.Command{
		Use:   "resolve <query>",
		Short: "Fuzzy-resolve a language name/code (e.g. chinees -> zh)",
		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			m, alts := lang.Resolve(args[0])
			kind := "fuzzy"
			if m.Exact {
				kind = "exact"
			}
			fmt.Printf("%s -> %s (%s) [%s, score %.2f]\n", args[0], m.Code, m.Name, kind, m.Score)
			for _, a := range alts {
				fmt.Printf("  ~ %s (%s) %.2f\n", a.Code, a.Name, a.Score)
			}
			return nil
		},
	})
	return c
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 11:49:19Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The `config path` command is working as intended—it just prints the path without actually creating the directory, which only happens when Load() runs during translate or pipe operations. But there's a critical bug where `./translate "hello, my friend" --to chinees` is being parsed incorrectly, treating "hello," as an unknown command instead of recognizing it as the input string.

The root cause is that once I added subcommands like `config` and `lang`, Cobra's default behavior changed—it now tries to match the first argument against known subcommands before passing anything to the root command's RunE handler. Since "hello, my friend" doesn't match any subcommand, Cobra errors out with "unknown command" instead of treating it as the text to translate. I need to configure Cobra to handle this disambiguation properly, either by adjusting how it processes arguments or by ensuring the root command's RunE runs even when the first arg isn't a recognized subcommand. I'm realizing the core issue: cobra's `legacyArgs` validator is what throws the "unknown command" error when a root command has subcommands but receives an argument that doesn't match any of them. The fix is to explicitly set the root command's `Args` field to allow arbitrary arguments, which bypasses that validation and lets the root's `RunE` handler process the input directly. Setting root.Args to cobra.ArbitraryArgs means bare text like "hello" gets passed to root.RunE instead of erroring, while subcommand routing still works normally through Find. The tradeoff is losing "did you mean" suggestions for typos at the top level, but that's acceptable for a translator where accepting arbitrary input is the priority. Let me apply this fix to root.go.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 11:49:19Z)**_

Found a real bug: adding subcommands made cobra reject bare text (`translate "hello"` → "unknown command"). This is the disambiguation issue — the fix is to set the root's `Args` to `ArbitraryArgs` so unknown first args fall through to translation while exact subcommands still route.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 11:50:13Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
All the slice 2 features are working as expected—config creation with hints, fuzzy matching for language codes like "chinees" to zh, default target from config, environment variable overrides, proper subcommand routing, and fuzzy matching for other languages like "japanes" to ja. I'm marking slice 2 complete and moving on to slice 3 for streaming one-shot translation. First, let me run gofmt to clean up the code.

Now for slice 3, I need to add streaming support. The plan is to refactor the Translate function in llm.go to handle streaming by checking the request flag, then POST with stream:true and parse the SSE response line by line, emitting ChunkToken events for each delta and ChunkDone when complete. In root.go, I'll enable streaming for the oneShot command when stdout is a TTY (unless --json is set), stream tokens directly to stdout, and add the --json flag to output the structured result instead. I'll also gate ANSI formatting based on whether stdout is a TTY.

Refactoring llm.go Translate...

I'm implementing the streaming logic by spawning a goroutine that handles both streaming and non-streaming paths through a channel. The function sets up the HTTP request with the appropriate Stream flag, then in the goroutine it reads the response—for streaming mode, it scans SSE lines, parses the delta payloads, accumulates tokens into a buffer, and sends each token through the channel; for non-streaming, it decodes the full response. Either way, it assembles the complete translation and sends ChunkDone with the final result.

Defining the streamDelta type...

I need to be careful about the 60-second client timeout—it applies to the entire request including body reads, which could theoretically cut off a long stream, but for translations that's acceptable. The SSE parsing handles both "data: {...}" with spaces and blank lines correctly by checking for the "data:" prefix and trimming whitespace. I should also guard against empty Choices arrays to avoid panics.

Now I'll refactor llm.go with the streaming implementation, then update root.go to add a --json flag and decide whether to stream based on whether output is a terminal and the flag isn't set.

Updating root.go...
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Looking at the v2 API for textarea and viewport components. The textarea exposes methods for dimensions, content management, focus handling, and cursor control with a declarative cursor model. The viewport similarly provides dimension and content setters along with a view method, suggesting a simpler interface focused on scrollable content display.

For the cursor behavior, I'm weighing whether to use the program-level cursor positioning or rely on the textarea's virtual cursor rendering. Since managing a real cursor across multiple widgets gets complex, I'll enable the textarea's virtual cursor mode so it draws its own styled block cursor within its view string rather than relying on the program-level cursor positioning.

Now I'm sketching out the TUI model structure: the engine, source and target strings, provider and model names for display, the textarea and viewport components, dimensions, a ready flag, status tracking (idle/translating/error), the translation result, error handling, and a quit flag. For messages, I'll have a result message that carries either the translation result or an error. During initialization, I'll focus the textarea and request the window size. In the update loop, I'll handle the window size message to set dimensions and mark ready, intercept keyboard input to bind Enter for translation while letting Alt+Enter pass through to the textarea for newlines, and route other keys to the textarea's update.

The translate command will call the engine's Translate method with streaming disabled, drain the result without a token callback, and return the result message. I'll store a base context in the model—for now using context.Background(), with cancellation support coming in a later slice. The view will stack the input box, result box, and footer vertically using lipgloss, and the layout will give the input about 4 lines with the result filling the remaining space.

I'm organizing the code into model.go (model and messages), update.go (update logic), view.go (view and styles), and keys.go (key bindings). Now I'm starting with the styles...

```go
package tui

import "charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"

// keyMap holds the TUI key bindings. It grows across slices; slice 4 uses the
// core three, later slices add live-toggle, engine cycle, language swap, etc.
type keyMap struct {
	Translate  key.Binding
	Newline    key.Binding
	ToggleLive key.Binding
	Clear      key.Binding
	Quit       key.Binding
}

func defaultKeys() keyMap {
	return keyMap{
		Translate:  key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("enter"), key.WithHelp("↵", "translate")),
		Newline:    key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"), key.WithHelp("⌥↵", "newline")),
		ToggleLive: key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+l"), key.WithHelp("^l", "live")),
		Clear:      key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+u"), key.WithHelp("^u", "clear")),
		Quit:       key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("ctrl+c", "esc"), key.WithHelp("^c", "quit")),
	}
}

```

```go
package tui

import lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"

// palette (fixed for now; light/dark adaptivity is a later slice).
var (
	colAccent = lg.Color("#7D56F4")
	colDim    = lg.Color("#6C6C6C")
	colErr    = lg.Color("#FF5F87")
	colOK     = lg.Color("#5FD787")
	colText   = lg.Color("#D0D0D0")
)

type styles struct {
	input   lg.Style
	inputHi lg.Style
	result  lg.Style
	footer  lg.Style
	label   lg.Style
	trans   lg.Style
	alt     lg.Style
	notes   lg.Style
	errText lg.Style
	dim     lg.Style
	liveOn  lg.Style
	liveOff lg.Style
}

func newStyles() styles {
	box := lg.NewStyle().Border(lg.RoundedBorder()).Padding(0, 1)
	return styles{
		input:   box.BorderForeground(colDim),
		inputHi: box.BorderForeground(colAccent),
		result:  box.BorderForeground(colDim),
		footer:  lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
		label:   lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colAccent).Bold(true),
		trans:   lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colText).Bold(true),
		alt:     lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
		notes:   lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim).Italic(true),
		errText: lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colErr),
		dim:     lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
		liveOn:  lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colOK),
		liveOff: lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
	}
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
// Package tui implements the interactive Bubble Tea (v2, charm.land/*) front-end.
// It imports the shared engine layer but the engine layer never imports it.
package tui

import (
	"context"

	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/textarea"
	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/viewport"
	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"

	"translate/internal/engine"
)

// Params configures a TUI session.
type Params struct {
	Engine     engine.Engine
	Source     string
	Target     string
	Provider   string // display name for the footer
	Model      string // display model id for the footer
	Live       bool   // live-debounce default state
	DebounceMs int
}

type status int

const (
	statusIdle status = iota
	statusTranslating
	statusDone
	statusError
)

// Model is the Bubble Tea model. All methods use value receivers and return the
// modified copy (the v2 convention) to avoid "state didn't stick" bugs.
type Model struct {
	p    Params
	base context.Context

	ta   textarea.Model
	vp   viewport.Model
	keys keyMap
	st   styles

	width, height int
	ready         bool

	source string
	target string
	live   bool

	status status
	result *engine.TranslateResult
	err    error

	// request lifecycle (used from slice 5 onward for debounce/cancel/stream)
	seq      int
	inflight int
	cancel   context.CancelFunc
}

// New builds a TUI model.
func New(ctx context.Context, p Params) Model {
	ta := textarea.New()
	ta.Placeholder = "Type text to translate…"
	ta.ShowLineNumbers = false
	ta.SetVirtualCursor(true)
	// Enter triggers translation, so move newline insertion to Alt+Enter.
	ta.KeyMap.InsertNewline = key.NewBinding(key.WithKeys("alt+enter"))
	ta.Focus()

	return Model{
		p:      p,
		base:   ctx,
		ta:     ta,
		vp:     viewport.New(),
		keys:   defaultKeys(),
		st:     newStyles(),
		source: p.Source,
		target: p.Target,
		live:   p.Live,
		status: statusIdle,
	}
}

// Init requests the initial window size and starts the cursor blink.
func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
	return tea.Batch(textarea.Blink, tea.RequestWindowSize)
}

// --- messages ---

// resultMsg carries a completed (non-streaming) translation. It is stamped with
// the request seq so stale results can be dropped once cancellation lands.
type resultMsg struct {
	seq int
	res *engine.TranslateResult
	err error
}

```


<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I'm working on the update.go file for the MVP, handling window size changes, keyboard input for translation/quit/clear operations, and result messages. For the translate command, I'll implement it as non-streaming for now—building a tea.Cmd that executes the translation and drains the result, then returns a resultMsg. I'm using the base context without per-request cancellation yet, though I'll add sequence numbers for correctness. Let me write out the update logic. Handling the remaining key bindings for clearing input, toggling live mode, and triggering translation, then implementing the launch function that validates the input text, increments a sequence counter, and spawns an async task to call the translation engine and return the result.

Now I'm realizing there's a receiver mismatch — handleKey has a value receiver but calls m.launch() which is a pointer method, so it modifies a copy. I should refactor launch to take a value receiver and return the modified model along with the command, making it consistent and cleaner. The same issue applies to relayout, which mutates textarea and viewport fields through pointer methods.

I'm working through the height calculation for the layout — accounting for borders on each box and ensuring the result viewport has enough space. Let me figure out the exact math for dividing the available height between the input box, result box, and footer.

Now I'm reconsidering the View function and realizing there's a width mismatch issue: if I set the textarea width to account for borders and padding, then apply a box style with its own width setting, things double-count. I need to pick one approach — either let the component handle its own width and let the box size to content, or set everything consistently through the style.

The cleaner solution is to set the component width to `m.width - 4` (subtracting border and padding), then have the style apply `.Width(m.width - 2)` which accounts for padding but lets the border add the final 2 pixels. This way the component renders at the right inner width, the style's width includes padding, and the border wraps everything to exactly `m.width`.

For height, I'll let the component drive it naturally — the box will be `inputH + 2` for the border with no vertical padding. Now I'm moving on to write the view logic with a status line showing the language pair, provider/model, and a live indicator, plus rendering the translation result. For the MVP I'll skip the spinner and just show "translating…" as placeholder text. Before I start writing the update and view files, I need to verify the key binding signature in the bubbles library — whether `key.Matches` accepts the right message type in the version I'm using.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 11:56:08Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"strings"

	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"

	"translate/internal/engine"
)

// Update handles messages. Value receiver returning the modified copy is the
// single convention used throughout (see Model doc).
func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	switch msg := msg.(type) {
	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
		m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
		m.relayout()
		m.ready = true
		return m, nil

	case tea.KeyPressMsg:
		return m.handleKey(msg)

	case resultMsg:
		if msg.seq != m.inflight {
			return m, nil // stale / superseded
		}
		m.inflight = 0
		if msg.err != nil {
			m.status = statusError
			m.err = msg.err
			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.errText.Render("✗ " + msg.err.Error()))
			return m, nil
		}
		m.status = statusDone
		m.err = nil
		m.result = msg.res
		m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*msg.res))
		return m, nil
	}

	var cmd tea.Cmd
	m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
	return m, cmd
}

func (m Model) handleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	switch {
	case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Quit):
		return m, tea.Quit
	case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLive):
		m.live = !m.live
		return m, nil
	case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Clear):
		m.ta.Reset()
		m.result = nil
		m.err = nil
		m.status = statusIdle
		m.vp.SetContent("")
		return m, nil
	case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Translate):
		return m.launch()
	}

	var cmd tea.Cmd
	m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
	return m, cmd
}

// launch starts a translation for the current input. In this slice it is
// non-streaming; slice 5 adds debounce, cancellation, and live token streaming.
func (m Model) launch() (Model, tea.Cmd) {
	text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
	if text == "" {
		m.status = statusIdle
		return m, nil
	}
	m.seq++
	m.inflight = m.seq
	m.status = statusTranslating
	m.err = nil

	seq := m.seq
	eng := m.p.Engine
	ctx := m.base
	req := engine.Request{
		Text:   text,
		Source: m.source,
		Target: m.target,
		Mode:   engine.ModeTranslate,
	}
	return m, func() tea.Msg {
		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
		if err != nil {
			return resultMsg{seq: seq, err: err}
		}
		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
		return resultMsg{seq: seq, res: res, err: err}
	}
}

// relayout recomputes component sizes for the current window. Widths account for
// each box's border (2) and horizontal padding (2).
func (m *Model) relayout() {
	if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
		return
	}
	const (
		footerH = 1
		inputH  = 4
	)
	compW := m.width - 4
	if compW < 1 {
		compW = 1
	}
	// height = input box (inputH+2 border) + result box (H+2 border) + footer
	resultH := m.height - footerH - (inputH + 2) - 2
	if resultH < 3 {
		resultH = 3
	}
	m.ta.SetWidth(compW)
	m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)
	m.vp.SetWidth(compW)
	m.vp.SetHeight(resultH)
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"

	"translate/internal/engine"
	"translate/internal/lang"
)

// View renders the two-box layout (input, result) plus a status/help footer.
func (m Model) View() tea.View {
	if !m.ready {
		return tea.NewView("initializing…")
	}
	inStyle := m.st.input
	if m.ta.Focused() {
		inStyle = m.st.inputHi
	}
	input := inStyle.Width(m.width - 2).Render(m.ta.View())
	result := m.st.result.Width(m.width - 2).Render(m.vp.View())
	footer := m.st.footer.Width(m.width).Render(m.statusLine())

	body := lg.JoinVertical(lg.Left, input, result, footer)
	return tea.NewView(body)
}

// statusLine renders the state segment and the key hints.
func (m Model) statusLine() string {
	pair := fmt.Sprintf("%s→%s", lang.Name(m.source), lang.Name(m.target))

	engineSeg := m.p.Provider
	if m.p.Model != "" {
		engineSeg = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", m.p.Model, m.p.Provider)
	}

	live := m.st.liveOff.Render("live○")
	if m.live {
		live = m.st.liveOn.Render("live●")
	}

	var state string
	switch m.status {
	case statusTranslating:
		state = m.st.dim.Render("…translating")
	case statusError:
		state = m.st.errText.Render("error")
	}

	left := strings.Join([]string{
		m.st.label.Render(pair),
		m.st.dim.Render(engineSeg),
		live,
	}, m.st.dim.Render(" · "))
	if state != "" {
		left += "  " + state
	}

	help := m.st.dim.Render("↵ translate  ^l live  ^u clear  ^c quit")
	return left + "  " + help
}

// renderResult formats a completed translation. In this slice the result is the
// plain translation; alternatives/notes render when a later slice populates them.
func (m Model) renderResult(res engine.TranslateResult) string {
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString(m.st.trans.Render(res.Translation))

	if res.DetectedSource != "" && (m.source == "" || m.source == "auto") {
		b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.dim.Render("detected: "+lang.Name(res.DetectedSource)))
	}
	for _, a := range res.Alternatives {
		b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.alt.Render("~ "+a))
	}
	if res.Notes != "" {
		b.WriteString("\n" + m.st.notes.Render("ⓘ "+res.Notes))
	}
	return b.String()
}

```

Now I'm moving to slice 5, which handles the core streaming behavior: debouncing keystrokes, canceling stale requests, and rendering tokens as they arrive live. I need to add message types for debounce ticks, stream chunks, completion, and errors, then redesign the model to handle a self-resubscribing reader that pulls from the engine's streaming channel.

Defining the streaming architecture... I'm checking that the HTTP request setup is cheap and non-blocking since the actual work happens in a spawned goroutine, so launching translations won't stall the main loop. For the debounce logic, each keystroke increments the sequence number and cancels any in-flight request, then arms a debounce timer that fires after a delay—if the sequence number still matches when it fires, I launch the translation with a new sequence number, so later keystrokes can invalidate it.

During streaming, I accumulate tokens into a buffer and display them directly as they arrive, then on completion render the final result. I'm also handling the case where live mode is off (only Enter triggers translation), making sure Enter always bypasses debounce, and adding a distinct "typing" status for UX. When the input becomes empty, I cancel any in-flight work and clear the display.

Now I'm setting up the message types and command helpers for debouncing and streaming, then updating the model to track the stream channel, buffer, and debounce duration.

Writing message types and commands...

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"time"

	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"

	"translate/internal/engine"
)

// debounceMsg fires debounce after the last keystroke. It carries the seq it was
// armed with; a newer keystroke bumps m.seq so the stale tick is ignored.
type debounceMsg struct{ seq int }

// streamMsg carries one engine Chunk (token, done, or error), stamped with the
// request's seq so cancelled/superseded events are dropped in O(1).
type streamMsg struct {
	seq   int
	chunk engine.Chunk
}

// armDebounce schedules a debounceMsg after d. tea.Tick fires exactly once, so N
// keystrokes arm N ticks; the seq guard collapses them to the last.
func armDebounce(seq int, d time.Duration) tea.Cmd {
	return tea.Tick(d, func(time.Time) tea.Msg { return debounceMsg{seq: seq} })
}

// waitStream reads the next Chunk from ch and wraps it as a seq-stamped msg. The
// engine contract guarantees a terminal ChunkDone/ChunkError before close, so a
// closed channel without one is surfaced as an error.
func waitStream(ch <-chan engine.Chunk, seq int) tea.Cmd {
	return func() tea.Msg {
		c, ok := <-ch
		if !ok {
			return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: engine.ErrNoResult}}
		}
		return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: c}
	}
}

```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm rewriting the update.go file to handle streaming with debouncing and cancellation. The Update function needs to process window resize messages for relayout, key presses through a handler, debounce messages that check sequence numbers before launching, and stream messages that validate the inflight sequence to avoid dropping stale tokens. The handleKey function will manage quit commands by canceling operations and returning tea.Quit.

Writing the update handlers... Writing handler logic... So the return type forwarding works fine in Go — Model satisfies tea.Model, so returning (Model, tea.Cmd) where (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) is expected is valid. I'll keep launch returning (Model, tea.Cmd) for clarity. Now I'm thinking through the goroutine lifecycle: when we cancel an in-flight request, the engine goroutine sees the context cancellation and closes the channel, so any stale waitStream command reading from the old channel gets dropped by the sequence guard. The potential issue is if the old waitStream is still blocked on a channel read when cancellation happens — once the goroutine's select fires on ctx.Done(), it closes the channel and the read unblocks.

When a stale token arrives with an old sequence number, handleStream returns without re-subscribing, so we stop draining that channel. The goroutine might block trying to send more tokens to its buffered channel, but since we cancelled, the select on ctx.Done() will fire and the goroutine exits, closing the channel. The old channel gets abandoned and garbage collected — no permanent goroutine leak. The model's stream field points to the new channel now, so the old one is only referenced by the resolved command, which drops it.

When Enter is called during a live translation with a pending debounce, bumping the sequence makes that debounceMsg stale and it gets ignored — preventing double translation. I'll update statusLine to show "…translating" for the translating state and leave typing blank to avoid flashing. Now I need to write the new update.go, making sure nothing else references the removed resultMsg.

Writing update.go...
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 12:02:03Z)**_

Now rewrite the Update loop for streaming, debounce, and cancellation:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 12:02:03Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"context"
	"strings"

	"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"

	"translate/internal/engine"
)

// Update handles messages. Value receiver returning the modified copy is the
// single convention used throughout (see Model doc).
func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	switch msg := msg.(type) {
	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
		m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
		m.relayout()
		m.ready = true
		return m, nil

	case tea.KeyPressMsg:
		return m.handleKey(msg)

	case debounceMsg:
		if msg.seq != m.seq {
			return m, nil // a newer keystroke arrived; this tick is stale
		}
		return m.launch()

	case streamMsg:
		return m.handleStream(msg)
	}

	var cmd tea.Cmd
	m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
	return m, cmd
}

// handleStream applies one engine Chunk, dropping anything from a superseded
// request. This seq guard is the correctness mechanism: cancelled results can
// never render even if a goroutine emits one late.
func (m Model) handleStream(msg streamMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	if msg.seq != m.inflight {
		return m, nil
	}
	switch msg.chunk.Kind {
	case engine.ChunkToken:
		m.streamBuf.WriteString(msg.chunk.Text)
		m.vp.SetContent(m.st.trans.Render(m.streamBuf.String()))
		m.vp.GotoBottom()
		return m, waitStream(m.stream, m.inflight) // re-subscribe for the next token

	case engine.ChunkDone:
		m.inflight = 0
		m.status = statusDone
		m.err = nil
		if msg.chunk.Result != nil {
			m.result = msg.chunk.Result
			m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*msg.chunk.Result))
		}
		return m, nil

	case engine.ChunkError:
		m.inflight = 0
		m.status = statusError
		m.err = msg.chunk.Err
		m.vp.SetContent(m.st.errText.Render("✗ " + msg.chunk.Err.Error()))
		return m, nil
	}
	return m, nil
}

func (m Model) handleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	switch {
	case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Quit):
		m.cancelInflight()
		return m, tea.Quit

	case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLive):
		m.live = !m.live
		return m, nil

	case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Clear):
		m.cancelInflight()
		m.seq++
		m.ta.Reset()
		m.result = nil
		m.err = nil
		m.streamBuf.Reset()
		m.status = statusIdle
		m.vp.SetContent("")
		return m, nil

	case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Translate):
		return m.launch()
	}

	// Ordinary keystroke: update the textarea, then (re)arm the debounce.
	var cmd tea.Cmd
	m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)

	if strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == "" {
		// Input cleared: cancel in-flight work and clear the result pane.
		m.cancelInflight()
		m.seq++
		m.result = nil
		m.streamBuf.Reset()
		m.status = statusIdle
		m.vp.SetContent("")
		return m, cmd
	}

	if m.live {
		m.seq++            // invalidate any pending debounce tick
		m.cancelInflight() // stop any in-flight request; its results become stale
		m.status = statusTyping
		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, armDebounce(m.seq, m.debounce))
	}
	return m, cmd
}

// launch starts a streaming translation for the current input, cancelling any
// in-flight request first. It is the single place a translation begins.
func (m Model) launch() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
	if text == "" {
		m.status = statusIdle
		return m, nil
	}
	m.seq++
	if m.cancel != nil {
		m.cancel()
	}
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
	m.cancel = cancel
	m.inflight = m.seq
	m.status = statusTranslating
	m.streamBuf.Reset()
	m.err = nil
	m.result = nil

	seq := m.seq
	req := engine.Request{
		Text:   text,
		Source: m.source,
		Target: m.target,
		Mode:   engine.ModeTranslate,
		Stream: true,
	}
	ch, err := m.p.Engine.Translate(ctx, req)
	if err != nil {
		e := err
		return m, func() tea.Msg {
			return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: e}}
		}
	}
	m.stream = ch
	return m, waitStream(ch, seq)
}

// cancelInflight cancels the current request (if any) and marks it as no longer
// in flight so late chunks are dropped.
func (m *Model) cancelInflight() {
	if m.cancel != nil {
		m.cancel()
		m.cancel = nil
	}
	m.inflight = 0
}

// relayout recomputes component sizes for the current window. Widths account for
// each box's border (2) and horizontal padding (2).
func (m *Model) relayout() {
	if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
		return
	}
	const (
		footerH = 1
		inputH  = 4
	)
	compW := m.width - 4
	if compW < 1 {
		compW = 1
	}
	resultH := m.height - footerH - (inputH + 2) - 2
	if resultH < 3 {
		resultH = 3
	}
	m.ta.SetWidth(compW)
	m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)
	m.vp.SetWidth(compW)
	m.vp.SetHeight(resultH)
}

```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The timing issue is resolved—the debounce and re-translation are working as expected, just needed sufficient latency for the API to respond. Slice 5 is confirmed working: live auto-translation triggers without requiring Enter, and edits properly supersede previous translations.

The core streaming mechanism is verified through the SSE plumbing and the TUI's waitStream path, so the incremental token rendering is in place. With the foundation solid across slices 1-5, I'm moving forward through the remaining slices to cover all four engines. Let me proceed to slice 6 and start implementing the fallback chain logic with Ollama support.

I'm building a Chain type that wraps multiple engines in priority order, caching availability probes with a TTL to avoid constant checks. The chain will implement the Engine interface, handling failover transparently — when translating, it filters candidates by language support, skips unavailable ones, and switches to the next engine if the current one fails before streaming any tokens. I'll also need a builder function that constructs the ordered engine list from config, mapping provider names to their corresponding LLM engines, with placeholders for the Google and dictionary engines coming in later slices. Writing chain implementation... Finishing the failover logic...

I'm realizing the availability probe adds latency on every translation—800ms every 5 seconds for the cache window. For the first keystroke, that's noticeable. I could optimize by skipping the probe for the first engine and just attempting it directly, using probes only to skip engines we know are down. This way the happy path stays snappy, and we only pay the probe cost when we need to decide on failover.

Implementing this with a recentlyDown check that skips engines marked as down within the cache TTL, then tries the engine directly. On connection errors, we mark it down and failover. Connection refused fails fast anyway, so failover is quick. This removes the pre-probe latency entirely while keeping the failover logic intact.

Implementing the recentlyDown helper and markDown/markUp tracking...

Now I need to handle the translation loop carefully: skip if recently down, try the engine, and handle different chunk types. On success we mark up, on errors we mark down and continue to the next engine. But there's a subtle issue with context cancellation—if the user cancels mid-request, we shouldn't failover to the next engine since the context is already dead. We should just stop and emit the cancellation error instead of wasting cycles trying every engine with a dead context.

I'll add a check at the start of each engine attempt and after the inner loop: if the context is cancelled, break out and return the error immediately rather than continuing to the next candidate. This way we cleanly distinguish between a real translation failure (which warrants failover) and an abandoned request (which should just stop).

Now I'm writing the chain.go implementation with this logic, and then moving to the builder in cmd to set up the engine configuration.

Building the engine factory... The result object already has the engine and model stamped, so I can track which engine actually served the request. I'll add fields to store the current engine and model, then update them when a translation completes. The footer will show the actual engine used if available, otherwise fall back to the configured provider and model. For the initial display in auto mode, I'll show the primary provider's model, and it'll update to reflect the actual engine once a result comes back.

Let me start implementing the chain builder.
</details></think>

```go
package engine

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"sync"
	"time"
)

// Chain is the AUTO fallback router. It tries engines in configured order and
// fails over to the next one when the current engine is known-down or fails
// before producing any token. Once tokens have streamed, a mid-stream error is
// surfaced rather than restarting on another engine (which would garble output);
// the common "provider not running" case fails at connect time, pre-token, and
// switches cleanly.
type Chain struct {
	engines []Engine
	ttl     time.Duration

	mu    sync.Mutex
	avail map[string]probeCache
}

type probeCache struct {
	ok bool
	at time.Time
}

// NewChain builds a chain from engines in priority order. ttl is how long a
// health verdict is cached (default 5s).
func NewChain(engines []Engine, ttl time.Duration) *Chain {
	if ttl <= 0 {
		ttl = 5 * time.Second
	}
	return &Chain{engines: engines, ttl: ttl, avail: map[string]probeCache{}}
}

// Name reports the chain as the "auto" engine.
func (c *Chain) Name() string { return "auto" }

// Supports reports true if any member supports the mode.
func (c *Chain) Supports(m Mode) bool {
	for _, e := range c.engines {
		if e.Supports(m) {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}

// recentlyDown reports whether we have a fresh "down" verdict for e, so we can
// skip it without paying a probe on the hot path. Engines are otherwise tried
// optimistically (no pre-probe latency on the happy path).
func (c *Chain) recentlyDown(e Engine) bool {
	c.mu.Lock()
	defer c.mu.Unlock()
	pc, ok := c.avail[e.Name()]
	return ok && !pc.ok && time.Since(pc.at) < c.ttl
}

func (c *Chain) mark(e Engine, ok bool) {
	c.mu.Lock()
	c.avail[e.Name()] = probeCache{ok: ok, at: time.Now()}
	c.mu.Unlock()
}

func (c *Chain) candidates(m Mode) []Engine {
	var out []Engine
	for _, e := range c.engines {
		if e.Supports(m) {
			out = append(out, e)
		}
	}
	return out
}

// Translate proxies the first working engine's stream. Failover happens only
// before the first token.
func (c *Chain) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
	cands := c.candidates(req.Mode)
	if len(cands) == 0 {
		return nil, ErrNoEngineForMode
	}
	out := make(chan Chunk, 32)
	go func() {
		defer close(out)
		var lastErr error
		for _, e := range cands {
			if ctx.Err() != nil { // request abandoned (e.g. user typed more)
				out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: ctx.Err()}
				return
			}
			if c.recentlyDown(e) {
				lastErr = fmt.Errorf("%s: recently unavailable", e.Name())
				continue
			}
			sub, err := e.Translate(ctx, req)
			if err != nil {
				lastErr = err
				c.mark(e, false)
				continue
			}

			streamed, failed := false, false
			for ch := range sub {
				switch ch.Kind {
				case ChunkToken:
					streamed = true
					out <- ch
				case ChunkDone:
					c.mark(e, true)
					out <- ch
					return
				case ChunkError:
					lastErr = ch.Err
					failed = true
					c.mark(e, false)
				}
			}

			if failed {
				if ctx.Err() != nil { // cancelled: don't waste other engines
					out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: ctx.Err()}
					return
				}
				if streamed { // already showed partial output: surface, don't restart
					out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: lastErr}
					return
				}
				continue // clean pre-token failover
			}
		}
		if lastErr == nil {
			lastErr = ErrAllEnginesFailed
		}
		out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%w: %v", ErrAllEnginesFailed, lastErr)}
	}()
	return out, nil
}

// Detect returns the first available engine's detection, else "".
func (c *Chain) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
	for _, e := range c.engines {
		if c.recentlyDown(e) {
			continue
		}
		if code, err := e.Detect(ctx, text); err == nil && code != "" {
			return code, nil
		}
	}
	return "", nil
}

// Available reports whether any member is currently reachable.
func (c *Chain) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
	for _, e := range c.engines {
		if e.Available(ctx) {
			c.mark(e, true)
			return true
		}
		c.mark(e, false)
	}
	return false
}

```

```go
package cmd

import (
	"fmt"
	"time"

	"translate/internal/config"
	"translate/internal/engine"
)

// buildEngine constructs the Engine for an invocation from resolved settings.
// engine="auto" builds a fallback Chain over the providers named in chain.order;
// a named provider builds a single LLM engine. (google/dict engines join the
// chain in later slices.)
func buildEngine(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
	cfg := res.Cfg

	if res.Engine != "auto" {
		if res.Provider == nil {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
		}
		return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
	}

	var engines []engine.Engine
	for _, name := range cfg.Chain.Order {
		if p := cfg.ProviderByName(name); p != nil {
			engines = append(engines, llmFromProvider(p, p.ModelForTier(res.Tier)))
		}
		// "google" / "dict" entries are wired in later slices.
	}
	if len(engines) == 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no engines available in chain.order (check %s)", config.Path())
	}
	if len(engines) == 1 {
		return engines[0], nil
	}
	return engine.NewChain(engines, 5*time.Second), nil
}

// llmFromProvider builds an LLM engine for a provider using the given model id.
func llmFromProvider(p *config.Provider, model string) *engine.LLMEngine {
	if model == "" {
		model = p.Model
	}
	return engine.NewLLM(engine.LLMConfig{
		Name:      p.Name,
		BaseURL:   p.BaseURL,
		Model:     model,
		APIKeyEnv: p.APIKeyEnv,
	})
}

```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
So the go-toml v2 configuration properly replaces the provider slice with just copilot and ollama, which is what we want for deterministic behavior. And the fallback chain is working as expected — when copilot fails to connect on port 4199, it falls through to ollama, which successfully responds with the llama3.2:3b model.

The config loading behavior is solid: if a user omits [[provider]] entirely, Load() starts from the defaults and keeps all three providers. If they specify any [[provider]] blocks, those replace everything. That's the expected behavior.

Now moving into slice 7 — implementing the Google engine with offline language detection. I'm building a GoogleEngine that hits the Google Translate API endpoint, parses the nested array response defensively, and extracts the detected source language from the response data. It's non-streaming and only supports translation mode. I'm also wiring in whatlanggo for offline language detection and integrating the "google" engine into the chain builder so it gets appended when enabled in the config.

The tricky part is handling DetectedSource across different engines. Google gives it for free from the API response, but the LLM engine returns an empty string since it doesn't parse detection. Rather than leave that gap, I can have the LLM engine import the lang package and fill in DetectedSource using offline detection when the source is auto and the result comes back empty. There's no import cycle since lang only depends on levenshtein, and the engine can safely import lang. Writing Google engine implementation... Handling the HTTP response and error cases, then decoding the JSON payload from Google's API. Parsing the response defensively to extract translated segments...

I notice the `segs` variable isn't being used, so I'll remove that. For the Detect method on GoogleEngine, I could make a network call but it's simpler to fall back to the offline language detection for now to avoid coupling the engine to the language detector. The Available method can return true optimistically since the chain already handles retries with recently-down tracking.

Now I'm adding language detection using whatlanggo and implementing a Detect function in the lang package. I need to wire the Google engine into the build chain when it's enabled, and fill in DetectedSource for the LLM engine by calling lang.Detect on the input text when the source is auto-detected. _**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 12:09:56Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package lang

import "github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo"

// Detect returns a best-effort ISO-639-1 source-language code for text, or "" if
// the guess is not confident. Used to fill DetectedSource on the LLM path (where
// the model returns only the translation) and as a fallback when APIs are down.
func Detect(text string) string {
	info := whatlanggo.Detect(text)
	if info.Confidence < 0.5 {
		return ""
	}
	return info.Lang.Iso6391()
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package engine

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"net/url"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"translate/internal/lang"
)

// GoogleConfig configures the free, unofficial Google translate_a/single endpoint.
type GoogleConfig struct {
	Endpoint  string
	ExtraDT   []string // extra dt slots, e.g. "bd" (bilingual dict), "at" (alternates)
	UserAgent string
	Timeout   time.Duration
}

// GoogleEngine is a keyless translation engine using Google's web endpoint.
// It is unofficial and best-effort: it can rate-limit (HTTP 429) or change shape
// without notice, so it lives at the end of the fallback chain.
type GoogleEngine struct {
	cfg  GoogleConfig
	http *http.Client
}

// NewGoogle builds a Google engine with sensible defaults.
func NewGoogle(cfg GoogleConfig) *GoogleEngine {
	if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
		cfg.Endpoint = "https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single"
	}
	if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
		cfg.Timeout = 4 * time.Second
	}
	if cfg.UserAgent == "" {
		cfg.UserAgent = "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli"
	}
	return &GoogleEngine{cfg: cfg, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
}

// Name returns "google".
func (e *GoogleEngine) Name() string { return "google" }

// Supports reports that Google handles translation only (not dictionary).
func (e *GoogleEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }

func (e *GoogleEngine) buildURL(req Request) string {
	sl := req.Source
	if sl == "" {
		sl = "auto"
	}
	q := url.Values{}
	q.Set("client", "gtx")
	q.Set("sl", sl)
	q.Set("tl", req.Target)
	q.Set("dt", "t")
	for _, dt := range e.cfg.ExtraDT {
		q.Add("dt", dt)
	}
	q.Set("q", req.Text)
	return e.cfg.Endpoint + "?" + q.Encode()
}

// Translate performs one keyless translation (non-streaming).
func (e *GoogleEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
	if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
		return nil, ErrEmptyInput
	}
	httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.buildURL(req), nil)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)

	resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
	if err != nil {
		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: %w", err)), nil
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
		body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))), nil
	}

	var data []json.RawMessage
	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&data); err != nil {
		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: decode: %w", err)), nil
	}
	res, err := parseGoogle(data, req)
	if err != nil {
		return single(nil, err), nil
	}
	res.Engine = e.Name()
	return single(res, nil), nil
}

// parseGoogle defensively walks the nested-array response. Positions: data[0] is
// an array of [translated, original, ...] segments (long text splits into many);
// data[2] is the detected source language.
func parseGoogle(data []json.RawMessage, req Request) (*TranslateResult, error) {
	if len(data) < 1 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: empty payload")
	}
	var segs []json.RawMessage
	if err := json.Unmarshal(data[0], &segs); err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: bad segments: %w", err)
	}
	var b strings.Builder
	for _, s := range segs {
		var parts []json.RawMessage
		if json.Unmarshal(s, &parts) != nil || len(parts) == 0 {
			continue
		}
		var txt string
		if json.Unmarshal(parts[0], &txt) == nil {
			b.WriteString(txt)
		}
	}
	res := &TranslateResult{Translation: strings.TrimSpace(b.String()), Target: req.Target}
	if res.Translation == "" {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: no translation in response")
	}
	if len(data) > 2 {
		var det string
		if json.Unmarshal(data[2], &det) == nil {
			res.DetectedSource = det
		}
	}
	return res, nil
}

// Detect uses the offline detector (cheap, no network round-trip).
func (e *GoogleEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
	return lang.Detect(text), nil
}

// Available probes the endpoint with a tiny translation request.
func (e *GoogleEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
	defer cancel()
	u := e.buildURL(Request{Text: "a", Source: "auto", Target: "en"})
	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
	if err != nil {
		return false
	}
	req.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
	resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
	if err != nil {
		return false
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
	return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
}

```

Looking at some test cases with whatlanggo on short text samples, I'm seeing it's quite unreliable—it's misidentifying languages like Spanish as Lithuanian and English as Dutch, with very low confidence scores even when it gets the language right. The library seems genuinely problematic for this use case.

Lowering the confidence threshold would only make things worse by surfacing these wrong detections instead of suppressing them. So keeping the 0.5 threshold is the right call—it means most short phrases won't have a detected_source on the LLM path, but that's acceptable since detection is really just a fallback bonus anyway. The Google engine provides accurate detection through its API, and the LLM path's structured output would need JSON to reliably capture the source language, which we deliberately avoided for streaming performance.

I'm going to stick with option 1: keep the 0.5 threshold and accept that short text often won't show detected_source. It's honest behavior—only show detection when confident. For longer text, whatlanggo becomes more reliable and will fill in the field. This is fine for v1.

Now I need to test the full fallback chain where both LLM engines are down and Google takes over. I'll update the config to kill both copilot and ollama endpoints, keep Google enabled, and verify the engine switches to Google.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Both verification tests are passing—the fallback to Google Translate works when the LLMs are down, correctly identifying the German text and its source language, and the LLM path also detects language properly for longer French text. Slice 7 is confirmed working, and the engine now supports multiple translation paths.

Moving to slice 8 for the dictionary engine, which needs to handle exact word lookups via the dictionaryapi.dev API and fall back to fuzzy matching when a word isn't found. The challenge is implementing the fuzzy resolution—I need a word list to find similar headwords when there's a 404, but bundling something like SCOWL would be too heavy. I'm considering either embedding a smaller word list via go:embed, or leveraging the system dictionary file if available (like /usr/share/dict/words on macOS with around 235k words), with a graceful fallback if the file isn't present on other systems.

For now, I'll add a Wordlist field to the Dict config that defaults to /usr/share/dict/words when empty, loads the word list lazily, and disables fuzzy matching if the file isn't found—keeping the binary lean while still working well on this machine. When a 404 occurs, I'll rank candidates by Levenshtein distance with a length prefilter for speed, then re-query the API with the nearest match if it's within a reasonable distance threshold.

Implementing the fuzzy index...

The DictEngine will parse the dictionary API response into a structured entry with word, phonetic, meanings, and source URLs. For the Translate method in dict mode, I'll lowercase and trim the input, fetch the entry, and if it's found return a result with the first definition as a gloss. If it's a 404 and fuzzy matching is enabled, I'll try the nearest word within distance 2 and fetch that instead, marking it as a fuzzy match. Otherwise return an error.

For the CLI, I'm adding a `define` subcommand that sets Mode=ModeDict and routes through the dict engine, with nice formatting for terminal output and --json support for structured results.

Now I'm realizing the routing issue: if the engine is set to auto and mode is dict, the chain filters candidates to those supporting ModeDict. But LLMEngine.Supports currently returns true for everything, which means it would be included before the dict engine. That's wrong because the LLM's Translate method doesn't handle dict mode—it would just translate the word instead of defining it. For v1, I'll restrict LLMEngine to only support ModeTranslate, so dict mode exclusively routes to the dictionary engine. This defers LLM-based definitions for now since the dictionary API is English-only anyway.

For the define command specifically, I'll have it bypass the chain entirely and instantiate the dict engine directly with ModeDict. This keeps the separation clean: the translate chain uses LLM/Google/Ollama (all translate-only), the dict engine stays dict-only, and the define command goes straight to the dictionary engine. I can safely keep "dict" in the chain order without it interfering with translate mode, since the candidates filter will exclude it.

Now I'm implementing the pieces: the DictEngine with word indexing, updating LLM.Supports to translate-only, building the define subcommand with CLI rendering, and adding a Dict.Wordlist config field. I'll use a default word list path like /usr/share/dict/words, with an edit distance of 2 for suggestions.

Writing the dictionary engine...
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package engine

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"os"
	"strings"
	"sync"
	"time"

	"github.com/agnivade/levenshtein"
)

// DictConfig configures the dictionary engine.
type DictConfig struct {
	Endpoint string // base, e.g. https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries
	Lang     string // e.g. "en"
	Fuzzy    bool
	Wordlist string // path to a newline word list; "" => /usr/share/dict/words
	Timeout  time.Duration
}

// DictEngine looks up word definitions via the Free Dictionary API, with an
// exact match first and a local fuzzy (nearest-headword) fallback on 404.
type DictEngine struct {
	cfg  DictConfig
	wl   *wordIndex
	http *http.Client
}

// NewDict builds a dictionary engine.
func NewDict(cfg DictConfig) *DictEngine {
	if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
		cfg.Endpoint = "https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries"
	}
	if cfg.Lang == "" {
		cfg.Lang = "en"
	}
	if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
		cfg.Timeout = 5 * time.Second
	}
	path := cfg.Wordlist
	if path == "" {
		path = "/usr/share/dict/words"
	}
	return &DictEngine{cfg: cfg, wl: &wordIndex{path: path}, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
}

// Name returns "dictionary".
func (e *DictEngine) Name() string { return "dictionary" }

// Supports reports that this engine handles dictionary lookups only.
func (e *DictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }

// Detect is not meaningful for the dictionary engine.
func (e *DictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) { return "", nil }

// Available probes the API with a common word.
func (e *DictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
	defer cancel()
	_, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, "test")
	return err == nil && status == http.StatusOK
}

// Translate performs a dictionary lookup (exact, then fuzzy on 404).
func (e *DictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
	word := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))
	if word == "" {
		return nil, ErrEmptyInput
	}

	entry, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, word)
	if err == nil && status == http.StatusOK && entry != nil {
		return single(e.result(entry, req, false, ""), nil), nil
	}
	if err != nil {
		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err)), nil
	}

	// 404 (or empty): try the nearest local headword.
	if status == http.StatusNotFound && e.cfg.Fuzzy {
		if cand, ok := e.wl.nearest(word, 2); ok && cand != word {
			if entry2, s2, e2 := e.fetch(ctx, cand); e2 == nil && s2 == http.StatusOK && entry2 != nil {
				return single(e.result(entry2, req, true, cand), nil), nil
			}
		}
	}
	return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word)), nil
}

func (e *DictEngine) result(entry *DictEntry, req Request, fuzzy bool, matched string) *TranslateResult {
	return &TranslateResult{
		Translation:  entry.gloss(),
		Target:       req.Target,
		Engine:       e.Name(),
		Dictionary:   entry,
		Fuzzy:        fuzzy,
		FuzzyMatched: matched,
	}
}

// --- Free Dictionary API wire types ---

type apiEntry struct {
	Word      string        `json:"word"`
	Phonetic  string        `json:"phonetic"`
	Phonetics []apiPhonetic `json:"phonetics"`
	Meanings  []apiMeaning  `json:"meanings"`
	SourceURLs []string     `json:"sourceUrls"`
}
type apiPhonetic struct {
	Text string `json:"text"`
}
type apiMeaning struct {
	PartOfSpeech string          `json:"partOfSpeech"`
	Definitions  []apiDefinition `json:"definitions"`
	Synonyms     []string        `json:"synonyms"`
	Antonyms     []string        `json:"antonyms"`
}
type apiDefinition struct {
	Definition string `json:"definition"`
	Example    string `json:"example"`
}

// fetch requests one word; returns (entry, httpStatus, err). A 404 yields
// (nil, 404, nil) so the caller can trigger the fuzzy fallback.
func (e *DictEngine) fetch(ctx context.Context, word string) (*DictEntry, int, error) {
	u := fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%s", strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.Endpoint, "/"), e.cfg.Lang, urlPathEscape(word))
	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, 0, err
	}
	resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, 0, err
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusNotFound {
		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
		return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
	}
	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
		body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
		return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))
	}
	var entries []apiEntry
	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&entries); err != nil {
		return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("decode: %w", err)
	}
	if len(entries) == 0 {
		return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
	}
	return mergeEntries(entries), http.StatusOK, nil
}

// mergeEntries folds the API's per-entry results into one DictEntry.
func mergeEntries(entries []apiEntry) *DictEntry {
	out := &DictEntry{Word: entries[0].Word}
	for _, en := range entries {
		if out.Phonetic == "" && en.Phonetic != "" {
			out.Phonetic = en.Phonetic
		}
		for _, p := range en.Phonetics {
			if out.Phonetic == "" && p.Text != "" {
				out.Phonetic = p.Text
			}
		}
		for _, m := range en.Meanings {
			meaning := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: m.PartOfSpeech, Synonyms: m.Synonyms, Antonyms: m.Antonyms}
			for _, d := range m.Definitions {
				meaning.Definitions = append(meaning.Definitions, Definition{Text: d.Definition, Example: d.Example})
			}
			out.Meanings = append(out.Meanings, meaning)
		}
		if out.SourceURL == "" && len(en.SourceURLs) > 0 {
			out.SourceURL = en.SourceURLs[0]
		}
	}
	return out
}

// gloss returns a short one-line definition for history/plain display.
func (d *DictEntry) gloss() string {
	for _, m := range d.Meanings {
		for _, def := range m.Definitions {
			if def.Text != "" {
				return def.Text
			}
		}
	}
	return d.Word
}

func urlPathEscape(s string) string {
	// dictionary words are simple; escape spaces defensively.
	return strings.ReplaceAll(s, " ", "%20")
}

// --- local fuzzy word index ---

type wordIndex struct {
	path  string
	once  sync.Once
	words []string
}

func (wi *wordIndex) load() {
	wi.once.Do(func() {
		b, err := os.ReadFile(wi.path)
		if err != nil {
			return // no wordlist: fuzzy silently disabled
		}
		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(b), "\n") {
			w := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(line))
			if w != "" {
				wi.words = append(wi.words, w)
			}
		}
	})
}

// nearest returns the closest word within maxDist edits, or ("", false).
func (wi *wordIndex) nearest(word string, maxDist int) (string, bool) {
	wi.load()
	best := ""
	bestD := maxDist + 1
	for _, w := range wi.words {
		if abs(len(w)-len(word)) > maxDist {
			continue
		}
		d := levenshtein.ComputeDistance(word, w)
		if d < bestD {
			bestD, best = d, w
			if d == 0 {
				break
			}
		}
	}
	if best != "" && bestD <= maxDist {
		return best, true
	}
	return "", false
}

func abs(n int) int {
	if n < 0 {
		return -n
	}
	return n
}

```

```go
package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"strings"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"

	"translate/internal/config"
	"translate/internal/engine"
)

func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
	return &cobra.Command{
		Use:     "define <word>",
		Aliases: []string{"dict"},
		Short:   "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy)",
		Args:    cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
		RunE:    runDefine,
	}
}

func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
	cfg, _, err := config.Load()
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
		return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
	}
	de := dictFromConfig(cfg)
	word := strings.Join(args, " ")

	ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict})
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	res, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	if flagJSON {
		enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
		return enc.Encode(res)
	}
	fmt.Print(renderDict(res))
	return nil
}

// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
	d := res.Dictionary
	if d == nil {
		return res.Translation + "\n"
	}
	var b strings.Builder
	if res.Fuzzy && res.FuzzyMatched != "" {
		b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("(no exact match — showing nearest: %s)\n", res.FuzzyMatched))
	}
	head := d.Word
	if d.Phonetic != "" {
		head += "  " + d.Phonetic
	}
	b.WriteString(head + "\n")
	for _, m := range d.Meanings {
		b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
		for i, def := range m.Definitions {
			if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
				break
			}
			b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
			if def.Example != "" {
				b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
			}
		}
	}
	return b.String()
}

```

```go
// Package store persists translation history. v1 uses an append-only JSONL file
// with in-memory fuzzy search behind the Store interface; a sqlite+FTS5 backend
// can drop in later without changing callers.
package store

import (
	"context"
	"time"
)

// Record is one history entry (also the JSONL line shape).
type Record struct {
	ID           string    `json:"id"`
	TS           time.Time `json:"ts"`
	SourceLang   string    `json:"source_lang"`
	TargetLang   string    `json:"target_lang"`
	Engine       string    `json:"engine"`
	Model        string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
	Input        string    `json:"input"`
	Output       string    `json:"output"`
	Alternatives []string  `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
	Notes        string    `json:"notes,omitempty"`
	Favorite     bool      `json:"favorite,omitempty"`
}

// Store is the history persistence interface.
type Store interface {
	// Add assigns an ID and timestamp, persists the record, and returns it.
	Add(ctx context.Context, r Record) (Record, error)
	// Recent returns up to limit records, newest first.
	Recent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]Record, error)
	// Search returns records fuzzy-matching query (over input+output), best first.
	Search(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Record, error)
	// Close releases any resources.
	Close() error
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package store

import (
	"context"
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"sync"
	"time"

	"github.com/sahilm/fuzzy"
)

// jsonlStore is an append-only JSONL-backed Store with in-memory fuzzy search.
type jsonlStore struct {
	path string
	mu   sync.Mutex
}

// OpenJSONL opens (creating parent dirs for) a JSONL history file.
func OpenJSONL(path string) (Store, error) {
	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o700); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return &jsonlStore{path: path}, nil
}

func (s *jsonlStore) Close() error { return nil }

// Add stamps the record with an id + timestamp and appends it as one JSON line.
func (s *jsonlStore) Add(ctx context.Context, r Record) (Record, error) {
	if r.ID == "" {
		r.ID = newID()
	}
	if r.TS.IsZero() {
		r.TS = time.Now()
	}
	line, err := json.Marshal(r)
	if err != nil {
		return r, err
	}

	s.mu.Lock()
	defer s.mu.Unlock()
	f, err := os.OpenFile(s.path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o600)
	if err != nil {
		return r, err
	}
	defer f.Close()
	if _, err := f.Write(append(line, '\n')); err != nil {
		return r, err
	}
	return r, nil
}

// readAll loads every record in chronological (file) order.
func (s *jsonlStore) readAll() ([]Record, error) {
	s.mu.Lock()
	defer s.mu.Unlock()
	b, err := os.ReadFile(s.path)
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
			return nil, nil
		}
		return nil, err
	}
	var out []Record
	for _, line := range strings.Split(string(b), "\n") {
		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
		if line == "" {
			continue
		}
		var r Record
		if json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &r) == nil {
			out = append(out, r)
		}
	}
	return out, nil
}

// Recent returns up to limit records, newest first.
func (s *jsonlStore) Recent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]Record, error) {
	all, err := s.readAll()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	reverse(all)
	if limit > 0 && len(all) > limit {
		all = all[:limit]
	}
	return all, nil
}

// Search fuzzy-ranks records by "input ⏎ output", best match first.
func (s *jsonlStore) Search(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Record, error) {
	all, err := s.readAll()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	q := strings.TrimSpace(query)
	if q == "" {
		reverse(all)
		if limit > 0 && len(all) > limit {
			all = all[:limit]
		}
		return all, nil
	}
	hay := make([]string, len(all))
	for i, r := range all {
		hay[i] = r.Input + " " + r.Output
	}
	matches := fuzzy.Find(q, hay)
	out := make([]Record, 0, len(matches))
	for _, m := range matches {
		out = append(out, all[m.Index])
		if limit > 0 && len(out) >= limit {
			break
		}
	}
	return out, nil
}

func reverse(rs []Record) {
	for i, j := 0, len(rs)-1; i < j; i, j = i+1, j-1 {
		rs[i], rs[j] = rs[j], rs[i]
	}
}

// newID is a sortable-enough unique id: base36 nanos + 4 random bytes.
func newID() string {
	var b [4]byte
	_, _ = rand.Read(b[:])
	return strconv.FormatInt(time.Now().UnixNano(), 36) + "-" + hex.EncodeToString(b[:])
}

```

```go
// Package state persists small session state (the last language pair and source
// mode) so a bare `translate` resumes where the user left off.
package state

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"time"

	"translate/internal/xdgpath"
)

// State is the persisted last-session snapshot.
type State struct {
	Source     string    `json:"source"`               // "auto" or a code
	Target     string    `json:"target"`               // code
	SourceMode string    `json:"source_mode,omitempty"` // auto | fixed
	Provider   string    `json:"provider,omitempty"`
	Engine     string    `json:"engine,omitempty"`
	UpdatedAt  time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
}

// Load reads the state file, returning a zero State if it does not exist.
func Load() (*State, error) {
	b, err := os.ReadFile(xdgpath.StateFile())
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
			return &State{}, nil
		}
		return nil, err
	}
	var s State
	if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &s); err != nil {
		return &State{}, nil // corrupt state is non-fatal
	}
	return &s, nil
}

// Save writes the state atomically (temp file + rename).
func Save(s *State) error {
	if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	s.UpdatedAt = time.Now()
	b, err := json.MarshalIndent(s, "", "  ")
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	p := xdgpath.StateFile()
	tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".state-*.json")
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	tmpName := tmp.Name()
	defer os.Remove(tmpName)
	if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
		tmp.Close()
		return err
	}
	if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-09 12:18:57Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I need to wire everything into the command layer. I'll open the store in runRoot and pass it down to both oneShot and the TUI, apply the last saved state to source and target before resolving the pair, then handle writing records and persisting state in each mode. I'm also adding a helper to construct store records from translation results and restructuring runRoot to coordinate all this. After a successful translation, I need to save the state and write to history. I'm creating a function that captures the source, target, and engine details into a record, handling the case where source is "auto" by using the detected language if available. Then in the oneShot flow, after getting the result, I'll write both the history record and update the saved state.

The key insight is that oneShot should return the result so that runRoot can handle the history and state writes consistently for both command-line arguments and stdin cases, while runTUI manages its own flow separately. I'm also adding a --no-history flag to gate whether history recording happens at all.

I'll create a helper function that checks if history is enabled and the store exists, then records the translation and optionally saves the last source/target pair if that's configured. For opening the store, I need to respect the History.Enabled setting and fall back to the default XDG path if none is specified, returning nil if anything fails so the rest of the program continues gracefully.

Now I need to refactor root.go to wire this all together—updating oneShot to return the result, modifying runRoot to call the record helper after oneShot completes, and adding the openStore function.
</details></think>
